profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact NGK or Denso plug numbers, 0.039-0.043in gap, and 13 lb-ft torque for the 2008 Honda Civic 1.8L R18A1.
extraKeywords: Honda Civic R18 spark plug torque
Honda Civic spark plug torque spec

supportNote.title: 2008 Honda Civic 1.8L spark plug jobs are about the gap first
supportNote.intro: The R18A1 engine uses iridium long-life plugs. The most common mistake is ordering copper replacements or setting the wrong gap.
bullets: Use NGK IZFR6K-11S or Denso SKJ20DR-M11S iridium plugs — copper replacements will foul early.
Gap must be 0.039–0.043 in (1.0–1.1 mm). Do not adjust iridium plug gaps by bending the ground electrode.
Torque to 13 lb-ft (18 N·m). The aluminum head strips easily — a torque wrench is mandatory.
The coils are coil-on-plug; disconnect the connector and remove the 10 mm bolt before pulling the coil straight up.
Apply a thin film of anti-seize to the plug threads and dielectric grease inside the coil boot.

faq.question: What spark plugs does a 2008 Honda Civic 1.8L take?
faq.answer: The 2008 Honda Civic with the 1.8L R18A1 engine uses NGK IZFR6K-11S or Denso SKJ20DR-M11S iridium long-life plugs. Gap is 0.039–0.043 in and torque is 13 lb-ft. Copper or platinum substitutes will foul faster and are not recommended by the factory service manual.

profile.descriptionSuffix: 0W-20 synthetic, 4.4 qt with filter, and the dash reset sequence for the 2013 Honda CR-V K24Z6.

supportNote.title: 2013 Honda CR-V oil changes are spec-sensitive
supportNote.intro: The K24Z6 engine requires 0W-20 full synthetic. Using 5W-20 or conventional oil can affect VTEC operation and fuel economy.
bullets: Use 0W-20 full synthetic API SN or newer. Capacity is 4.4 US qt (4.2 L) with filter replacement.
The drain plug torque is 29 lb-ft (40 N·m) and uses a 17 mm socket. Crush washer should be replaced.
Oil filter is a spin-on canister at the front of the engine. Torque to 11 lb-ft (15 N·m) by hand plus a quarter turn.
Reset the maintenance minder: hold the trip button, turn key to ON (not start), hold until the oil life resets to 100%.
Maintenance Minder code A1 or B1 typically appears every 7,500–10,000 miles depending on driving conditions.

faq.question: How much oil does a 2013 Honda CR-V take?
faq.answer: The 2013 Honda CR-V with the 2.4L K24Z6 engine takes 4.4 US quarts (4.2 liters) of 0W-20 full synthetic motor oil when replacing the filter. The factory recommends API SN or newer grade. Always check the dipstick after starting and add if needed.

profile.descriptionSuffix: NGK DILZKR7A11G iridium plugs, 0.044 in gap, 13 lb-ft torque for the 2013 Honda CR-V K24Z6.

supportNote.title: 2013 Honda CR-V spark plugs are iridium-only
supportNote.intro: The K24Z6 uses fine-wire iridium plugs with a specific ground strap design. Copper or platinum substitutes will cause misfires under load.
bullets: Use NGK DILZKR7A11G or Denso SXU22HCR11S iridium plugs. Gap is 0.044 in (1.1 mm) — do not adjust.
Torque to 13 lb-ft (18 N·m). The cylinder head is aluminum — over-torquing will strip the threads.
Coil-on-plug design: remove the 10 mm coil bolt, disconnect the harness, and pull straight up with a twisting motion.
Inspect coil boots for oil contamination — a leaking valve cover gasket will fill the spark plug tube with oil.
Apply dielectric grease inside the coil boot and a small dab of anti-seize on plug threads before installation.

faq.question: What spark plugs go in a 2013 Honda CR-V?
faq.answer: The 2013 Honda CR-V with the 2.4L K24Z6 engine uses NGK DILZKR7A11G or Denso SXU22HCR11S iridium long-life spark plugs. The factory gap is 0.044 in (1.1 mm) and torque is 13 lb-ft. Do not use copper or platinum replacements — they will foul and cause misfires.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Front 13.03 in or 13.78 in rotors depending on brake package, 20 lb-ft caliper bolt torque for 2012 Ford F-150.

supportNote.title: 2012 Ford F-150 brakes split by package code
supportNote.intro: The 12th-gen F-150 uses either standard or heavy-duty brake packages. Ordering the wrong pad shape or rotor diameter will leave you mid-job without the right parts.
bullets: Check the brake package code on the door jamb sticker. 54D = heavy duty (13.78 in front rotors), 54C = standard (13.03 in).
Front caliper bracket bolts torque to 85 lb-ft (115 N·m). Caliper slide bolts are 20 lb-ft (27 N·m).
The front pads use an anti-rattle clip — transfer the old clips or install new ones with the pad set.
Compress the piston with a C-clamp or piston tool. If the piston is seized, the caliper may need replacement.
Bed the new pads: 10 gradual stops from 30 mph, then 10 firm stops from 45 mph without coming to a complete stop.

faq.question: What size brake rotors does a 2012 Ford F-150 have?
faq.answer: The 2012 Ford F-150 has either 13.03-inch (standard) or 13.78-inch (heavy-duty) front brake rotors depending on the brake package code. Check the door jamb sticker for code 54C (standard) or 54D (heavy duty). Rear rotors are typically 13.03 inches on most configurations.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Denso SC20HR11 iridium plugs, 0.043 in gap, 13 lb-ft torque for the 2010 Toyota Corolla 1.8L.

supportNote.title: 2010 Toyota Corolla 1.8L uses long-life iridium plugs
supportNote.intro: The 2ZR-FE engine runs fine-wire iridium plugs with a 120,000-mile service interval. The biggest risk is using copper replacements or setting the gap wrong.
bullets: Use Denso SC20HR11 or NGK LKR6D-10E iridium plugs. Gap is 0.043 in (1.1 mm) — do not adjust iridium electrodes.
Torque to 13 lb-ft (18 N·m). The aluminum cylinder head is easy to strip — always use a torque wrench.
Coil-on-plug layout: remove the 10 mm coil bolt, disconnect the harness lock tab, and pull the coil straight up.
If a coil boot is stuck, twist gently — do not yank. A torn boot will cause misfires and need replacement.
Apply dielectric grease inside the coil boot and anti-seize on plug threads. Service interval is 120,000 miles.

faq.question: What spark plugs does a 2010 Toyota Corolla 1.8L take?
faq.answer: The 2010 Toyota Corolla with the 1.8L 2ZR-FE engine uses Denso SC20HR11 or NGK LKR6D-10E iridium long-life spark plugs. The factory gap is 0.043 in (1.1 mm) and torque is 13 lb-ft. These plugs have a 120,000-mile service interval. Copper or platinum substitutes are not recommended.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Group 51R, 410–500 CCA, top-post battery with the positive terminal on the right side for 2010 Honda CR-V.

supportNote.title: 2010 Honda CR-V battery fitment is about group size first
supportNote.intro: The CR-V uses a Group 51R battery in a tight tray. The wrong group size or terminal layout will not fit the hold-down or cables.
bullets: Use Group 51R with 410–500 CCA minimum. The positive terminal is on the right (passenger) side when facing the battery.
The hold-down bracket uses a J-bolt and 10 mm nut. Remove it before lifting the battery — the tray is plastic and cracks easily.
Disconnect negative first, then positive. Reconnect positive first, then negative to avoid short circuits.
No battery registration is required on the 2010 CR-V, but radio presets and clock will reset.
Clean the cable terminals with a wire brush and apply terminal protector spray after installation.

faq.question: What battery size fits a 2010 Honda CR-V?
faq.answer: The 2010 Honda CR-V uses a Group 51R battery with a minimum of 410–500 cold cranking amps (CCA). The positive terminal is on the right side when facing the battery. Popular fits include DieHard Gold 51R, Interstate MTX-51R, and Optima YellowTop D51R.

profile.titleSuffix: Water Pump Location & Timing Belt Note
profile.descriptionSuffix: Behind the timing cover on the EJ25 SOHC — replace the timing belt, tensioner, and thermostat at the same time.

supportNote.title: 2007 Subaru Outback water pump is behind the timing belt
supportNote.intro: The EJ25 SOHC water pump is driven by the timing belt. This is not a standalone job — the timing belt, tensioner, and idlers should all be replaced together.
bullets: The water pump sits behind the timing cover and is driven by the timing belt. Plan for a full timing belt kit, not just the pump.
Torque the water pump bolts to 8.7 lb-ft (11.8 N·m) — over-torquing will crack the pump housing or warp the gasket.
Use Subaru Super Coolant (blue) or an equivalent phosphate-free, silicate-free long-life coolant. Capacity is ~7.4 qt.
Replace the thermostat and radiator hoses while the system is drained and the timing cover is off.
The crank pulley bolt requires a breaker bar or impact wrench — it is torqued to 132 lb-ft (180 N·m).

faq.question: Is the water pump behind the timing belt on a 2007 Subaru Outback?
faq.answer: Yes. The 2007 Subaru Outback with the EJ25 SOHC engine has the water pump behind the timing cover and driven by the timing belt. Replacing the water pump requires removing the timing belt, so Subaru recommends replacing the timing belt, tensioner, and idler pulleys at the same time.

profile.descriptionSuffix: NGK LKR6D-10E iridium plugs, 0.039–0.043 in gap, 14.5 lb-ft torque for the 2012 Hyundai Elantra 1.8L.

supportNote.title: 2012 Hyundai Elantra 1.8L uses iridium long-life plugs
supportNote.intro: The Nu engine runs NGK iridium plugs with a 105,000-mile service interval. The coil-on-plug design is straightforward, but the aluminum head is easy to strip.
bullets: Use NGK LKR6D-10E or Denso SC20HR11 iridium plugs. Gap is 0.039–0.043 in (1.0–1.1 mm).
Torque to 14.5 lb-ft (20 N·m). The aluminum cylinder head strips at higher torque — a torque wrench is essential.
Remove the plastic engine cover first — it is held by four push clips and two 10 mm nuts.
Coil bolts are 10 mm. Disconnect the harness clip by squeezing the lock tab, then pull the coil straight up.
Apply anti-seize to plug threads and dielectric grease inside the coil boot. Service interval is 105,000 miles.

faq.question: What spark plugs does a 2012 Hyundai Elantra 1.8L take?
faq.answer: The 2012 Hyundai Elantra with the 1.8L Nu engine uses NGK LKR6D-10E or Denso SC20HR11 iridium long-life spark plugs. The factory gap is 0.039–0.043 in and torque is 14.5 lb-ft. The recommended service interval is 105,000 miles.

profile.descriptionSuffix: 5W-20 synthetic blend, 4.5 qt with filter, Motorcraft FL-400-S for the 2012 Ford Fusion 2.5L.

supportNote.title: 2012 Ford Fusion 2.5L oil changes are straightforward but spec-specific
supportNote.intro: The 2.5L Duratec uses 5W-20 and a cartridge-style filter. Using the wrong weight or a spin-on filter adapter will cause problems.
bullets: Use 5W-20 synthetic blend or full synthetic API SN. Capacity is 4.5 US qt (4.3 L) with filter.
The filter is a cartridge type (Motorcraft FL-400-S) under a black plastic cap on top of the engine. Torque the cap to 19 lb-ft (25 N·m).
Drain plug is 13 mm and torques to 20 lb-ft (27 N·m). Replace the crush washer.
The oil life monitor resets through the message center: Settings > Vehicle > Oil Life > Hold OK to Reset.
Ford recommends 7,500-mile intervals under normal conditions. Severe duty (towing, short trips) drops to 5,000 miles.

faq.question: How much oil does a 2012 Ford Fusion 2.5L take?
faq.answer: The 2012 Ford Fusion with the 2.5L Duratec engine takes 4.5 US quarts (4.3 liters) of 5W-20 motor oil when replacing the cartridge filter. Ford recommends API SN or newer synthetic blend or full synthetic. Always verify the level on the dipstick after the first start.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Under the intake manifold on the 1.6L EcoBoost — plan for intake gasket and coolant line removal.

supportNote.title: 2013 Ford Fusion 1.6L EcoBoost starter is under the intake
supportNote.intro: Unlike most transverse four-cylinders, the 1.6L EcoBoost buries the starter under the intake manifold. This job requires intake removal and adds significant labor time.
bullets: The starter is located under the intake manifold on the transmission side of the 1.6L EcoBoost engine.
Intake manifold removal is required. Replace the intake gasket and inspect the coolant crossover tube O-rings.
Disconnect battery negative first. The starter motor is heavy — support it before removing the last bolt.
The starter bolts torque to 30 lb-ft (40 N·m). Clean the mounting surface for good ground contact.
If the starter clicks but does not crank, test battery voltage first, then check the starter signal wire at the S-terminal.

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2013 Ford Fusion 1.6L EcoBoost?
faq.answer: The starter on the 2013 Ford Fusion with the 1.6L EcoBoost engine is located under the intake manifold on the transmission side of the engine. Replacing it requires removing the intake manifold, so plan for a new intake gasket and inspect the coolant crossover tube O-rings while the intake is off.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Upper housing at the front of the 5.0L Coyote — 18 lb-ft housing torque and a specific bleed screw sequence.

supportNote.title: 2011 Ford F-150 5.0L thermostat sits in the upper housing
supportNote.intro: The Coyote 5.0L places the thermostat in the upper radiator hose housing at the front of the engine. Access is good, but the bleed procedure is specific and must be followed to avoid air pockets.
bullets: The thermostat is in the housing where the upper radiator hose meets the engine, front passenger side.
Housing bolts torque to 18 lb-ft (24 N·m). Use a new gasket — the old one will leak.
Use Motorcraft VC-7-B or equivalent yellow prediluted coolant. Capacity is ~17.1 qt (16.2 L) for the 5.0L.
Bleed procedure: fill the degas bottle to the cold fill line, run the engine to 200°F with the heater on max, then open the bleed screw on the thermostat housing until coolant flows steadily.
If the temperature gauge spikes after replacement, an air pocket is trapped. Re-bleed through the screw on the thermostat housing.

faq.question: Where is the thermostat on a 2011 Ford F-150 5.0L?
faq.answer: The thermostat on the 2011 Ford F-150 with the 5.0L Coyote V8 is located in the upper radiator hose housing at the front of the engine on the passenger side. The housing is held by two bolts torqued to 18 lb-ft. After replacement, bleed air through the bleed screw on the thermostat housing with the engine at operating temperature and the heater on max.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Lower housing on the 1.6L 7A-FE — 14 lb-ft torque, 170°F thermostat, and a simple two-bolt housing.

supportNote.title: 1996 Toyota Corolla 1.6L thermostat is in the lower housing
supportNote.intro: The 7A-FE engine places the thermostat in the lower radiator hose housing at the front of the block. This is one of the easiest thermostat jobs — only two bolts and minimal coolant loss.
bullets: The thermostat sits in the housing where the lower radiator hose connects to the engine block, front center.
Use a 170°F (76°C) thermostat. The 7A-FE runs cool — a 180°F or 192°F thermostat will trigger the check engine light.
Housing bolts are 10 mm and torque to 14 lb-ft (19 N·m). Use a new gasket.
Drain only the radiator first — about 2.5 qt will come out. Total system capacity is 5.3 qt.
Use Toyota Red Long Life or equivalent ethylene-glycol coolant. Do not mix green and red coolant.

faq.question: Where is the thermostat on a 1996 Toyota Corolla 1.6L?
faq.answer: The thermostat on the 1996 Toyota Corolla with the 1.6L 7A-FE engine is located in the lower radiator hose housing at the front of the engine block. The housing is held by two 10 mm bolts torqued to 14 lb-ft. Use a 170°F (76°C) thermostat — a higher temperature thermostat will trigger the check engine light on this engine.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Step-by-step oil change instructions for the 2015 Toyota Camry 2.5L 2AR-FE with exact torque specs and reset procedure.
extraKeywords: 2015 Toyota Camry oil change torque specs
2015 Toyota Camry maintenance required light reset
Toyota Camry 2AR-FE oil filter part number
how to reset oil light 2015 Toyota Camry

supportNote.title: Use 0W-20 synthetic and torque the drain plug to 30 lb-ft
supportNote.intro: The 2015 Toyota Camry with the 2.5L 2AR-FE engine requires a specific oil grade, capacity, and filter type to maintain warranty compliance and engine longevity.
bullets: The 2015 Toyota Camry 2.5L 2AR-FE engine requires 0W-20 full synthetic motor oil with a total capacity of 4.6 quarts when replacing the oil filter.
The oil filter is a spin-on canister, and the genuine Toyota part number is 04152-YZZA1; an equivalent aftermarket spin-on filter may also be used.
The drain plug must be tightened to exactly 30 lb-ft (40 Nm) to prevent leaks without damaging the aluminum oil pan threads.
To reset the Maintenance Required light, set the trip meter to display Trip A, then hold the ODO/Trip button while turning the ignition key to the ON position until the display counts down to zero.
Always start the engine after the oil and filter change, check for leaks at the drain plug and filter, verify the oil level on the dipstick, and top off if necessary.

faq.question: What oil does a 2015 Toyota Camry 2.5L take and how much?
faq.answer: The 2015 Toyota Camry with the 2.5L 2AR-FE engine takes 0W-20 full synthetic motor oil, and the total capacity is 4.6 quarts (4.4 liters) when the oil filter is replaced. Always use a spin-on canister filter such as the Toyota 04152-YZZA1 or a direct equivalent, and tighten the drain plug to 30 lb-ft.

profile.titleSuffix: 2020 Ford F-150 Oil Change by Engine
profile.descriptionSuffix: Capacities and weights for 5.0L V8, 2.7L EcoBoost, and 3.5L EcoBoost with cartridge filter location.
extraKeywords: 2020 F-150 Intelligent Oil Life Monitor reset

supportNote.title: 2020 Ford F-150 oil change — know your engine before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2020 Ford F-150 uses a cartridge-style oil filter mounted on the top of the engine, and capacities differ significantly by powertrain.
bullets: The 5.0L V8 engine requires 8.8 quarts of 5W-20 motor oil, while the 2.7L and 3.5L EcoBoost engines both require 6.0 quarts of 5W-30 motor oil.
The oil filter is a cartridge-type unit located on the top of the engine; replace it with a Motorcraft FL-2062 or equivalent cartridge.
The drain plug torque spec for the 2020 F-150 is 17 lb-ft; over-torquing can strip the aluminum oil pan threads.
Reset the Intelligent Oil Life Monitor by navigating to the oil life display with the steering wheel arrow buttons, then press and hold the OK button until the display resets to 100 percent.
Allow the engine to cool for at least 15 minutes before draining to avoid burn injuries and to let oil settle for a complete drain.

faq.question: How many quarts of oil does a 2020 Ford F-150 take?
faq.answer: It depends on the engine. The 5.0L V8 takes 8.8 quarts of 5W-20, while both the 2.7L EcoBoost and 3.5L EcoBoost take 6.0 quarts of 5W-30. Always verify with the dipstick after filling and running the engine briefly.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Step-by-step for 10th-gen Accord with EPB service mode, wear sensor routing, and 79 lb-ft bracket torque.
extraKeywords: Honda Accord 1.5L turbo front rotor size
2018 Accord electronic parking brake service mode
10th gen Accord brake caliper torque specs
Accord brake pad wear sensor replacement
2018 Honda Accord rear brake pad EPB reset

supportNote.title: 2018–2022 Accord 10th Gen Brake Pad Replacement
supportNote.intro: The 10th-generation Accord uses an electronic parking brake on the rear calipers and a brake pad wear sensor on the front inner pad, so the job differs from older cable-actuated models.
bullets: Front brake rotors measure 12.3 inches on the 1.5L turbo and 12.7 inches on the 2.0L turbo, so verify your engine code before ordering parts.
The caliper bracket bolts must be torqued to exactly 79 lb-ft; do not guess, because bracket movement will destroy pads and rotors.
The rear electronic parking brake must be placed in service mode through the dash menu or with a scan tool before compressing the rear caliper piston.
The front inner pad contains the brake pad wear sensor, which routes through the pad backing plate and must be transferred carefully to the new pad to keep the dash warning system functional.
After rear pad installation, exit service mode and cycle the EPB several times to confirm the actuator seats the pads against the rotor.

faq.question: Do you have to put the 2018 Honda Accord electronic parking brake in service mode to change rear brake pads?
faq.answer: Yes, the rear caliper piston on the 2018 Accord is actuated by an electric motor, so you must enter EPB service mode through the instrument-panel Vehicle Settings menu or with an OBD-II scan tool before compressing the piston. Attempting to force the piston in without service mode can strip the internal gears. After installing the new pads and reinstalling the caliper, exit service mode and apply the parking brake a few times to seat the pads.

profile.descriptionSuffix: 2014 Ford Escape battery replacement guide with Group 96R, 590 CCA specs, torque values, and EcoBoost BMS reset steps.
extraKeywords: 2014 Ford Escape battery location driver side
how to replace battery 2014 Ford Escape
2014 Ford Escape battery hold down bolt size

supportNote.title: Use a Group 96R battery with 590 CCA and reset the BMS on EcoBoost models
supportNote.intro: The 2014 Ford Escape requires a specific battery size and, on EcoBoost engines, a battery monitoring system reset to avoid charging system warnings.
bullets: The 2014 Ford Escape uses a Group 96R battery rated at 590 cold cranking amps with a standard top-post terminal layout.
The battery is located in the engine bay on the driver side, secured by a hold-down clamp bolt that should be torqued to 89 in-lb (10 Nm).
Torque the battery terminal nuts to 97 in-lb (11 Nm) and always connect the positive cable first, followed by the negative cable.
If your 2014 Escape has an EcoBoost engine, perform a Battery Monitoring System reset with a compatible scan tool after installation to recalibrate the charging strategy.
Failure to reset the BMS after battery replacement on EcoBoost models can trigger false low battery warnings and reduce alternator life.

faq.question: What size battery does a 2014 Ford Escape take?
faq.answer: A 2014 Ford Escape takes a Group 96R battery with 590 cold cranking amps (CCA) and a top-post terminal configuration. The battery is located in the engine bay on the driver side. If your Escape has an EcoBoost engine, you must perform a Battery Monitoring System (BMS) reset with a scan tool after installation so the powertrain control module learns the new battery state and avoids false low-voltage warnings.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2000 Nissan Maxima.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2000 Nissan Maxima with the VQ30DE 3.0L V6 carries its alternator on the front passenger side of the engine, tucked beneath the power steering reservoir. Factory units are 110A and use a dual-bolt mount with an automatic spring tensioner.
bullets: Alternator location: front passenger side, 110A rating (OE Nissan 23100-2Y900)
Mounting bolt torque: upper and lower M8x1.25 bolts, 18 ft-lb (25 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner pulley bolt clockwise to release tension on the 6-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 2-pin oval connector (S and L terminals) plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: failing to disconnect the negative battery cable before loosening the B+ nut, which can short the wrench to the intake manifold

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2000 Nissan Maxima?
faq.answer: On the 2000 Nissan Maxima, the alternator is mounted on the front passenger side of the engine, driven by a 6-rib serpentine belt, and is rated at 110 amps.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2015 Honda Odyssey.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2015 Honda Odyssey uses the J35Z8 3.5L V6 with the alternator positioned low on the front passenger side, just beneath the power steering pump. The combination of a long through-bolt and an upper adjusting stud requires careful sequencing during removal.
bullets: Alternator location: front lower passenger side, 130A rating (OE 31100-RV0-A01 or Remy 94113)
Mounting bolt torque: through-bolt (long horizontal bolt) 33 ft-lb (45 Nm), upper adjusting bolt 16 ft-lb (22 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner clockwise with a 14mm socket and long breaker bar to release the 6-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 3-pin rectangular weatherpack connector (FR, IG, L terminals) plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: not supporting the engine from below with a jack and wood block before loosening the upper torque rod, which is required for belt removal clearance

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2015 Honda Odyssey?
faq.answer: On the 2015 Honda Odyssey, the alternator is located on the front lower passenger side of the J35Z8 3.5L V6 engine, is rated at 130 amps, and is secured by a long through-bolt and an upper adjusting bolt.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2001 Nissan Pathfinder.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2001 Nissan Pathfinder with the VG33E 3.3L V6 uses a manually tensioned alternator mounted on the passenger side of the engine. Unlike later models, there is no automatic spring tensioner—you must loosen the pivot and adjuster bolts to slacken the belt.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front, 110A rating (OE Nissan 23100-0W800)
Mounting bolt torque: pivot bolt (M10x1.25) 33 ft-lb (45 Nm), adjuster lock nut (M8) 17 ft-lb (23 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: manual tensioner—loosen the pivot bolt and adjuster bolt, then turn the adjuster bolt clockwise to tighten the belt to 8-10mm deflection at 10 lbf
Electrical connector type: 2-pin T-connector (S and L terminals) plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: stripping the cast-aluminum adjustment bracket threads by overtightening the adjuster bolt beyond 17 ft-lb

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2001 Nissan Pathfinder?
faq.answer: On the 2001 Nissan Pathfinder, the alternator is mounted on the passenger side front of the VG33E 3.3L V6 engine, is rated at 110 amps, and uses a manual tensioner with a pivot bolt and separate adjuster bolt.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2010 Subaru Forester.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2010 Subaru Forester with the EJ253 2.5L naturally aspirated boxer engine places its alternator high on the passenger side of the engine, above the air conditioning compressor. Access is tight against the frame rail, so a universal joint on your ratchet is essential.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side top/front, 90A rating base (110A optional) (OE 23700-AA04A)
Mounting bolt torque: M8x1.25 bolts, 18 ft-lb (25 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: automatic tensioner is located on the driver side; rotate the tensioner arm clockwise with a 12mm socket to release the 4-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 3-pin white connector (IG, S, L terminals) plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: forgetting to reinstall the ground wire eyelet (10mm) that bolts to the rear alternator bracket, which causes intermittent charging and dash warning lights

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2010 Subaru Forester?
faq.answer: On the 2010 Subaru Forester, the alternator is located on the passenger side top/front of the EJ253 2.5L engine, is rated at 90 amps (110 amps on some trims), and is mounted with two M8x1.25 bolts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2010 Kia Soul.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2010 Kia Soul carries its alternator on the front passenger side of the Gamma 1.6L or Beta II 2.0L engine. Because the radiator hose and coolant reservoir sit directly above the upper bolt, most technicians remove the reservoir first for clearance.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front, 90A rating (OE 37300-2B101)
Mounting bolt torque: 14mm head M8 bolts, 18 ft-lb (25 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner clockwise with a 14mm wrench to release the 4-rib or 6-rib belt depending on trim
Electrical connector type: 2-pin connector plus 12mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: attempting to remove the alternator without first detaching the coolant reservoir (one 10mm bolt and hose clamp), which blocks upper bolt access and leads to cracked reservoir ears

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2010 Kia Soul?
faq.answer: On the 2010 Kia Soul, the alternator is mounted on the front passenger side of the engine, is rated at 90 amps, and is secured by two 14mm-head M8 bolts beneath the coolant reservoir.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2013 Ford Fusion.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2013 Ford Fusion with the 2.5L Duratec inline-4 mounts its alternator on the passenger side of the transverse engine. Because the engine sits sideways, passenger side means toward the front of the vehicle, and the alternator is accessible from above or through the passenger wheel well after removing the splash shield.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front of transverse engine, 150A rating (OE DG1T-10300-BB)
Mounting bolt torque: M8 bolts, 18 ft-lb (25 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner clockwise with a 3/8" drive ratchet inserted into the tensioner arm square hole to release the 6-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 2-pin oval connector plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: damaging the plastic wiring harness retainer clip on the rear cover of the alternator, which can allow the B+ cable to chafe against the AC line and short to ground

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2013 Ford Fusion?
faq.answer: On the 2013 Ford Fusion, the alternator is located on the passenger side front of the transverse 2.5L engine, is rated at 150 amps, and can be accessed from above or through the passenger wheel well.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2014 Ford Fusion.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2014 Ford Fusion carries over the same transverse layout as 2013, with the alternator on the passenger side front of the engine. On 2.0L EcoBoost models, the charge air cooler plumbing runs close to the alternator, so hose support is critical during service.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front of transverse engine, 150A rating (2.0L EcoBoost OE DG1Z-10346-A)
Mounting bolt torque: M8 bolts, 18 ft-lb (25 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner clockwise with a 3/8" drive ratchet to release the 6-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 2-pin oval connector plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: on 1.5L and 2.0L EcoBoost models, failing to support the charge air cooler hoses while working in the passenger wheel well, which can crack the plastic intercooler pipe connections

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2014 Ford Fusion?
faq.answer: On the 2014 Ford Fusion, the alternator is located on the passenger side front of the transverse engine, is rated at 150 amps on 2.0L EcoBoost models, and is secured by two M8 bolts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2010 Land Rover LR2.
extraKeywords: land rover lr2 alternator torque spec

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2010 Land Rover LR2 is built on the Volvo P1 platform and uses the B6324S 3.2L inline-6 engine. The alternator sits on the passenger side front of the engine, and a secondary air injection bracket often blocks removal unless it is detached first.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front, 150A rating (OE LR002902 or Volvo 36050262)
Mounting bolt torque: M8 bolts, 18 ft-lb (25 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner clockwise with a T50 Torx bit or 15mm wrench to release the 6-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 3-pin connector plus 13mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: forgetting to remove the secondary air injection pump bracket bolt (13mm) mounted above the alternator rear ear, which prevents the unit from sliding out of its mount

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2010 Land Rover LR2?
faq.answer: On the 2010 Land Rover LR2, the alternator is mounted on the passenger side front of the B6324S 3.2L inline-6 engine, is rated at 150 amps, and requires removal of the secondary air injection bracket for extraction.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2015 Honda Civic.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2015 Honda Civic with the R18Z1 1.8L inline-4 places its alternator low on the front passenger side, directly beneath the power steering pump on models so equipped and just above the oil filter on all trims. The long through-bolt requires a deep 14mm socket and patience to extract past the frame rail.
bullets: Alternator location: front lower passenger side, 95A rating (1.8L OE 31100-R1A-A01)
Mounting bolt torque: through-bolt (long horizontal bolt) 33 ft-lb (45 Nm), adjusting bolt 16 ft-lb (22 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner clockwise with a 14mm socket and breaker bar to release the 6-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 3-pin connector (FR, IG, L terminals) plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: rounding the 14mm tensioner bolt hex by using a 12-point socket or short ratchet—always use a 6-point socket and at least an 18-inch breaker bar for safe release

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2015 Honda Civic?
faq.answer: On the 2015 Honda Civic, the alternator is located on the front lower passenger side of the R18Z1 1.8L engine, is rated at 95 amps, and is held by a long through-bolt and an upper adjusting bolt.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2003 Toyota Corolla.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2003 Toyota Corolla with the 1ZZ-FE 1.8L engine uses a manually adjusted alternator mounted on the passenger side front of the engine. Unlike newer Corollas, there is no automatic spring tensioner—you must loosen the pivot bolt and turn the adjuster bolt to slacken the belt.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front, 80A rating (OE 27060-0D080)
Mounting bolt torque: pivot bolt (M10) 32 ft-lb (44 Nm), adjusting bolt (M8) 13 ft-lb (18 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: manual tensioner—loosen the 12mm pivot bolt, then back off the 12mm adjuster bolt on the slotted bracket to slacken the belt
Electrical connector type: 3-pin connector (S, L, IG terminals) plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: attempting to turn the adjuster bolt without first loosening the pivot bolt, which strips the fine-pitch threads in the aluminum adjustment bracket

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2003 Toyota Corolla?
faq.answer: On the 2003 Toyota Corolla, the alternator is mounted on the passenger side front of the 1ZZ-FE 1.8L engine, is rated at 80 amps, and uses a manual tensioner with a pivot bolt and adjuster bolt on a slotted bracket.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2001 Ford Escape.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2001 Ford Escape offers two engines: the 2.0L Zetec inline-4 and the 3.0L Duratec V6. Both mount the alternator on the passenger side front of the engine, but the 3.0L requires removal of the passenger engine mount bracket to access the upper alternator bolt.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front, 110A (2.0L Zetec) or 130A (3.0L Duratec) (OE YL8Z-10346-AA)
Mounting bolt torque: M8 bolts, 18 ft-lb (25 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner clockwise with a 3/8" drive ratchet to release the 6-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 2-pin connector plus 10mm B+ terminal nut

Common mistake: on the 3.0L Duratec, not removing the passenger side engine mount nut and bracket (15mm and 18mm fasteners) before attempting to extract the upper alternator bolt, which is blocked by the mount ear

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2001 Ford Escape?
faq.answer: On the 2001 Ford Escape, the alternator is located on the passenger side front of the engine and is rated at 110 amps on the 2.0L Zetec or 130 amps on the 3.0L Duratec V6.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2003 Honda Pilot.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2003 Honda Pilot with the J35A4 3.5L V6 carries its alternator low on the front passenger side, beneath the power steering pump and behind the engine mount bracket. Because this is a heavier truck application, the through-bolt torque is significantly higher than on car-based Honda V6 platforms.
bullets: Alternator location: front lower passenger side, 130A rating (OE 31100-PVJ-A01)
Mounting bolt torque: through-bolt (M10) 47 ft-lb (64 Nm), adjusting bolt 16 ft-lb (22 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: rotate automatic tensioner clockwise with a 14mm socket and breaker bar to release the 6-rib belt
Electrical connector type: 3-pin connector (FR, IG, L terminals) plus 10mm B+ terminal nut

Common mistake: attempting to remove the alternator without first removing the power steering pump bracket bolt (12mm) and the dipstick tube support bracket, which blocks the alternator from sliding rearward out of its mount

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2003 Honda Pilot?
faq.answer: On the 2003 Honda Pilot, the alternator is located on the front lower passenger side of the J35A4 3.5L V6 engine, is rated at 130 amps, and requires removal of the power steering pump bracket for extraction.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2014 Dodge Journey.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2014 Dodge Journey with the 3.6L Pentastar V6 places its alternator on the passenger side front of the engine. The alternator is one of the higher-amperage units in this generation, and the rear mounting bolt is partially hidden behind the oil filter cap support bracket on some builds.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front, 160A rating (3.6L Pentastar OE 56029698AA)
Mounting bolt torque: M10x1.5 bolts, 40 ft-lb (54 Nm)

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2014 Dodge Journey?
faq.answer: On the 2014 Dodge Journey, the alternator is located on the passenger side front of the engine, is rated at 160 amps on the 3.6L Pentastar V6, and uses M10x1.5 mounting bolts torqued to 40 ft-lb.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 1996 Jeep Cherokee.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 1996 Jeep Cherokee XJ with the 4.0L AMC inline-6 uses a time-tested manual tensioner design. The alternator sits high on the passenger side of the engine, and belt tension is set by a slotted adjustment bracket rather than an automatic spring tensioner.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side upper, 90A standard or 117A with heavy-duty/tow package (OE 56027924 or 56027925)
Mounting bolt torque: pivot bolt 33 ft-lb (45 Nm), adjustment bracket nut 15 ft-lb (20 Nm)
Serpentine belt routing note: manual tensioner—loosen the 1/2" or 13mm pivot bolt, then turn the 3/8" adjuster bolt clockwise to tighten the belt to 1/2" deflection at 10 lbf on the longest span
Electrical connector type: 2-pin field connector plus 10mm B+ terminal nut
Common mistake: overtightening the plastic-bodied adjustment bracket or cracking the cast aluminum adjuster ear by forcing the bolt beyond the bracket's travel limit

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 1996 Jeep Cherokee?
faq.answer: On the 1996 Jeep Cherokee, the alternator is mounted on the passenger side upper area of the 4.0L inline-6 engine and is rated at 90 amps (117 amps with the heavy-duty or tow package), using a manual tensioner with a slotted adjustment bracket.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact alternator position, amperage rating, mounting bolt torque, and serpentine belt routing for the 2005 Hyundai Tucson.

supportNote.title: Check amperage rating and belt tension before ordering
supportNote.intro: The 2005 Hyundai Tucson with the 2.0L Beta II or 2.7L Delta V6 mounts its alternator on the front passenger side of the engine. On the 2.0L, the upper bolt is partially obscured by the coolant reservoir, and most technicians find removal easier after detaching the reservoir.
bullets: Alternator location: passenger side front, 90A (2.0L) or 110A (2.7L) (OE 37300-23500 or 37300-37800)

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2005 Hyundai Tucson?
faq.answer: On the 2005 Hyundai Tucson, the alternator is mounted on the front passenger side of the engine and is rated at 90 amps on the 2.0L or 110 amps on the 2.7L V6, secured by two M8 bolts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact battery group size, cold cranking amps, terminal layout, and replacement steps for the 2008 Nissan Pathfinder.

supportNote.title: Use the correct group size battery with the right CCA rating
supportNote.intro: The 2008 Nissan Pathfinder uses a top-terminal flooded battery mounted on the passenger side of the engine bay. Access requires removing the battery cover on some trims, and the hold-down bracket sits over the front edge of the battery case.
bullets: Group size: 24F (10.3" L x 6.8" W x 8.9" H); CCA: 600–650 A
Terminal orientation: positive on the left when facing the battery from the front of the vehicle
Hold-down bracket bolt torque: 13 ft-lb (18 N·m)
BMS/ECU reset procedure: not required; no battery monitoring system on this model year
Common mistake: overtightening the J-bolt and cracking the battery tray flange

faq.question: What size battery does a 2008 Nissan Pathfinder take?
faq.answer: The 2008 Nissan Pathfinder takes a Group 24F battery with a cold cranking amps (CCA) rating of 600–650 A. The positive terminal is on the left side when facing the battery, and the hold-down bracket bolt should be tightened to 13 ft-lb.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact battery group size, cold cranking amps, terminal layout, and replacement steps for the 2008 Honda Civic.

supportNote.title: Use the correct group size battery with the right CCA rating
supportNote.intro: The 2008 Honda Civic (8th generation) uses a compact battery mounted on the passenger side firewall. Its reverse-polarity layout differs from many other vehicles, so terminal orientation is critical to avoid reversed-cable installation.
bullets: Group size: 51R (9.3" L x 5.0" W x 8.8" H); CCA: 410–500 A
Terminal orientation: positive on the right when facing the battery from the front of the vehicle
Hold-down bracket bolt torque: 9 ft-lb (12 N·m)
BMS/ECU reset procedure: not required; simply reset the clock and radio presets after reconnecting
Common mistake: installing a Group 51 (non-R) battery with reversed terminals, causing cable reach issues

faq.question: What size battery does a 2008 Honda Civic take?
faq.answer: The 2008 Honda Civic takes a Group 51R battery with a cold cranking amps (CCA) rating of 410–500 A. The positive terminal is on the right side when facing the battery, and the hold-down bracket bolt should be tightened to 9 ft-lb.

supportNote.title: Use the correct group size battery with the right CCA rating

profile.titleSuffix: Brake Pad Size, Torque & Caliper Specs
profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact front/rear pad fitment, rotor diameter, caliper bolt torque, and bed-in procedure for the 2010 Ford Fusion.

supportNote.title: Match pad size to rotor diameter and torque caliper bolts correctly
supportNote.intro: Pad shape on the Fusion corresponds directly to rotor diameter; using the wrong pad or under-torquing the bracket bolts can cause uneven wear and noise. Always verify rotor size before ordering pads and torque all fasteners to spec.
bullets: Front rotor diameter: 278 mm (10.9 in) on base trim; 302 mm (11.9 in) on Sport
Rear rotor diameter: 278 mm (10.9 in) on all trims
Front caliper bracket bolt torque: 110 Nm (81 lb-ft)
Caliper slide pin torque: 27 Nm (20 lb-ft)
Bed-in procedure: 10 stops from 60 mph to 10 mph with firm pedal pressure, allowing 30 seconds between stops for cooling

faq.question: What size brake pads does a 2010 Ford Fusion take?
faq.answer: The 2010 Ford Fusion takes front pads matched to either 278 mm or 302 mm rotors depending on trim; rear pads fit 278 mm rotors across all trims. Base and mid-level trims use 278 mm front rotors, while the Sport trim upgrades to 302 mm front rotors with a correspondingly larger pad. Always measure your rotor before ordering replacement pads.

faq.answer: The 2014 Nissan Altima takes front pads sized for 296 mm ventilated rotors and rear pads sized for 292 mm solid rotors. These are distinct pad shapes front and rear, so verify axle position when ordering replacement pads.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact low-beam, high-beam, and DRL bulb numbers, housing access, and 10-30 min replacement steps for the 2012 Toyota Tacoma.

supportNote.title: Use the correct bulb type and never touch the glass
supportNote.intro: The 2012 Toyota Tacoma uses separate halogen capsules for low and high beam. Base models have reflector housings, while up-level trims may use projector low beams. Access is straightforward from the engine bay by removing the rear dust covers.
bullets: Low beam bulb: H11 (55W, PGJ19-2 base) halogen capsule
High beam bulb: 9005 (HB3, 65W, P20d base) halogen capsule
DRL function: uses the high-beam 9005 bulb at reduced voltage; no separate DRL bulb
Access: engine bay, twist the rubber dust cover counterclockwise to remove
Retainer: bayonet-style twist lock; torque by hand only, no tools required

faq.answer: The 2012 Toyota Tacoma takes an H11 halogen bulb for the low beam and a 9005 (HB3) halogen bulb for the high beam. Base models use reflector housings, and some trims use projector low beams, but the bulb numbers remain H11 and 9005.

Retainer: plastic twist-lock ring; rotate counterclockwise 1/4-turn to release each bulb

faq.question: What headlight bulb does a 2014 Honda Odyssey take?
faq.answer: The 2014 Honda Odyssey takes an H11 halogen bulb for the low beam and a 9005 (HB3) halogen bulb for the high beam. This applies to both standard multi-reflector and Touring Elite projector headlight housings.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact low-beam, high-beam, and DRL bulb numbers, housing access, and 10-30 min replacement steps for the 2014 Toyota RAV4.

supportNote.title: Use the correct bulb type and never touch the glass
supportNote.intro: The 2014 Toyota RAV4 (fourth generation) uses projector headlights with separate 9005 halogen capsules for both low and high beam. The same bulb type is used in both positions, giving a uniform beam color and easy parts stocking.
bullets: Low beam bulb: 9005 (HB3, 65W, P20d base) halogen capsule
High beam bulb: 9005 (HB3, 65W, P20d base) halogen capsule
DRL: uses the high-beam 9005 circuit at reduced voltage on XLE and Limited trims
Access: engine bay, twist the rubber dust cover counterclockwise and pull it straight back
Retainer: plastic twist-lock ring on both low and high beam; rotate 1/4-turn counterclockwise to release

faq.question: What headlight bulb does a 2014 Toyota RAV4 take?
faq.answer: The 2014 Toyota RAV4 takes a 9005 (HB3) halogen bulb for both the low beam and high beam. Each projector headlight uses two separate 9005 capsules—one for low beam and one for high beam.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact low-beam, high-beam, and DRL bulb numbers, housing access, and 10-30 min replacement steps for the 2015 Ford Taurus.

supportNote.title: Use the correct bulb type and never touch the glass
supportNote.intro: The 2015 Ford Taurus uses either a single bi-beam halogen capsule or a D3S HID bulb depending on trim. SE and SEL trims equip projector halogen headlights with a 9012 (HIR2) bulb that handles both low and high beam. Limited and SHO trims use D3S HID projectors.
bullets: Low and high beam bulb (halogen): 9012 (HIR2, 55W, PX22d base) single bi-beam halogen capsule
HID-equipped Limited and SHO models: D3S (35W, PK32d-5 base) xenon discharge bulb
DRL: halogen models use the 9012 at reduced voltage; HID models use a separate LED strip
Access: engine bay, remove the plastic access panel from the rear of each headlight housing
Retainer: twist-lock ring; rotate counterclockwise 1/4-turn to release the 9012 or D3S bulb

faq.question: What headlight bulb does a 2015 Ford Taurus take?
faq.answer: The 2015 Ford Taurus takes a 9012 (HIR2) bi-beam halogen bulb on SE and SEL trims, which handles both low and high beam in one capsule. Limited and SHO trims with factory HID headlights use a D3S xenon bulb instead.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact low-beam, high-beam, and DRL bulb numbers, housing access, and 10-30 min replacement steps for the 2003 Ford Ranger.

supportNote.title: Use the correct bulb type and never touch the glass
supportNote.intro: The 2003 Ford Ranger uses a single dual-beam halogen bulb per headlight assembly. The 9007 (HB5) two-filament capsule provides both low and high beam in a reflector housing. Access is from the engine bay behind a rear cover.
bullets: Low and high beam bulb: 9007 (HB5, 65W high / 55W low, PX29t base) dual-filament halogen
No separate low-beam or high-beam bulb; the 9007 handles both functions
DRL: not applicable on base XL and XLT trims; Edge trims may use the 9007 at reduced voltage for DRL
Access: engine bay, remove the rectangular plastic cover from the rear of each headlight
Retainer: plastic twist-lock ring; rotate counterclockwise 1/4-turn to release the 9007 bulb

faq.question: What headlight bulb does a 2003 Ford Ranger take?
faq.answer: The 2003 Ford Ranger takes a 9007 (HB5) dual-beam halogen bulb. This single bulb provides both the low beam and high beam for each headlight assembly.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 1999 Nissan Altima.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: On the 1999 Nissan Altima with the 2.4L KA24DE engine, the starter sits on the passenger-side lower rear of the engine block, bolted to the transmission bellhousing. Access is tight against the firewall, so a long extension and universal joint are essential.
bullets: Exact location: passenger-side lower rear, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just above the axle (RH side)
Mounting bolt torque spec: 29–36 ft-lb (39–49 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bolts
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal first and wait 3 minutes before touching the B+ cable
Wiring harness clip locations: one 12-mm nut secures the B+ cable to the solenoid; the S-terminal uses a push-on blade connector with a plastic lock tab
Common access obstacle: the downstream O2 sensor harness and heat shield often block wrench swing—remove the shield’s two 10-mm bolts (Step 3 in FSM)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 1999 Nissan Altima?
faq.answer: The starter on a 1999 Nissan Altima is located on the passenger-side lower rear of the engine, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just above the right-hand axle. You reach it from under the vehicle or by reaching around the firewall side; a long extension with a universal joint helps clear the axle and exhaust.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2004 Nissan Sentra.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2004 Nissan Sentra (B15) with the 1.8L QG18DE or 2.5L QR25DE carries the starter on the driver’s-side front of the engine, low and bolted to the transmission case. You can reach it from above or below depending on which engine you have.
bullets: Exact location: driver’s-side front lower, bolted to the transmission bellhousing (1.8L) or left-rear of block (2.5L QR25DE)
Mounting bolt torque spec: 29–36 ft-lb (39–49 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bellhousing bolts
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and cover it; the B+ stud is live until the cable is removed
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 12-mm nut; S-terminal is a quick-connect spade behind a snap-lock cover
Common access obstacle: on the 2.5L SE-R, the upper bolt is hidden behind the alternator bracket—loosen the alternator’s two 14-mm bolts and swing it forward 2 inches (FSM Step 4)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2004 Nissan Sentra?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2004 Nissan Sentra is on the driver’s-side lower front of the engine, bolted to the transmission bellhousing. On the 2.5L QR25DE SE-R models it sits slightly farther back behind the alternator bracket, so you may need to swing the alternator forward for bolt access.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2012 Nissan Pathfinder.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2012 Nissan Pathfinder (R51) with the 4.0L VQ40DE V6 hides the starter on the passenger-side lower rear of the engine, tucked against the transmission bellhousing. A front skid plate and frame crossmember can obstruct the view on 4WD models.
bullets: Exact location: passenger-side lower rear, bolted to the transmission bellhousing above the right front axle shaft
Mounting bolt torque spec: 32–40 ft-lb (43–54 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bellhousing bolts
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal first; the B+ cable at the solenoid remains hot until removed
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable is held by a 13-mm nut; S-terminal uses a push-on connector with a locking tab on the integrated solenoid
Common access obstacle: on 4WD models, remove the front skid plate (four 12-mm bolts) and the exhaust Y-pipe heat shield (three 10-mm bolts) per FSM Section EM

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2012 Nissan Pathfinder?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2012 Nissan Pathfinder is located on the passenger-side lower rear of the engine, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just above the right front axle shaft. On 4WD models you will need to remove the front skid plate and exhaust heat shield to see it clearly.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2013 Ford Explorer.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: On the 2013 Ford Explorer (U502), the starter is mounted on the passenger-side lower engine block at the transmission bellhousing. Both the 3.5L Ti-VCT V6 and the 2.0L EcoBoost use the same general location, though bolt angles differ slightly.
bullets: Exact location: passenger-side (RH) lower engine block, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just behind the catalytic converter
Mounting bolt torque spec: 18 lb-ft (24 N·m) for the two M10 starter bolts; do not exceed 25 lb-ft
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and wait 1 minute for PCM capacitors to discharge before removing the B+ cable
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 13-mm nut on the solenoid; the S-terminal signal wire uses a push-lock connector facing the rear of the vehicle
Common access obstacle: the right-hand catalytic converter and its heat shield block direct access—remove the shield’s two 8-mm bolts and lower it 1 inch (Workshop Manual Section 303-06)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2013 Ford Explorer?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2013 Ford Explorer is on the passenger-side lower engine block, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just behind the right-hand catalytic converter. You will need to lower the catalytic converter heat shield and reach in from under the vehicle or through the wheel well.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2013 Nissan Altima.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2013 Nissan Altima (L33) with the 2.5L QR25DE positions the starter on the driver’s-side front lower engine, bolted to the transmission case. The engine mount and underbody splash shield are the main access hurdles.
bullets: Exact location: driver’s-side front lower, bolted to the transmission bellhousing near the half-shaft seal
Mounting bolt torque spec: 32–40 ft-lb (43–54 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bolts; use a torque wrench because the aluminum transmission case threads strip at 50 ft-lb
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and tuck the cable away; the B+ stud is always live
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 12-mm nut; S-terminal is a push-on blade with a plastic lock tab; a ground strap clip sits on the upper bolt boss

Common access obstacle: the plastic underbody splash shield and the engine mount rubber block the lower bolt—remove the splash shield (six push-pins) and support the engine with a jack before loosening the mount (FSM Section EM)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2013 Nissan Altima?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2013 Nissan Altima is on the driver’s-side front lower engine, bolted to the transmission bellhousing near the half-shaft seal. Remove the plastic underbody splash shield and support the engine before loosening the nearby engine mount for full access.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2008 Honda Fit.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: On the 2008 Honda Fit (GE8) with the 1.5L L15A7 engine, the starter sits on the front of the engine block, driver’s side, tucked beneath the intake manifold and throttle body. It is reachable from above once the air cleaner duct is out of the way.
bullets: Exact location: front of engine, driver’s-side lower, behind the throttle body and below the intake manifold runner
Mounting bolt torque spec: 32 ft-lb (44 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 mounting bolts; the long upper bolt and short lower bolt share the same torque
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal first; wait 3 minutes so the ECM can power down before disconnecting the B+ cable

Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable is secured with a 12-mm nut; the S-terminal uses a blade connector with a locking tab; a small harness clip on the starter body must be pried off with a flathead

Common access obstacle: the intake air duct and resonator box block the top view—remove the duct (two Phillips screws and one hose clamp) and the battery hold-down bracket (10-mm bolts) per Service Manual page 4-7

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2008 Honda Fit?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2008 Honda Fit is on the front of the engine, driver’s-side lower, tucked beneath the intake manifold and throttle body. Remove the intake air duct and resonator box to see the two mounting bolts and wiring connectors clearly.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2000 Honda Civic.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2000 Honda Civic (6th generation) with the 1.6L D-series engine places the starter on the front of the block, passenger side, directly below the exhaust manifold. It is one of the most accessible Honda starters, reachable from above or below.
bullets: Exact location: front of engine, passenger-side lower, below the exhaust manifold and above the oil filter on D16Y7/D16Y8 engines
Mounting bolt torque spec: 32 ft-lb (44 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bolts; the upper bolt is long, the lower bolt is short
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and tuck it away from the post; the B+ cable nut is exposed and live until removed
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 12-mm nut on the solenoid; the S-terminal is a push-on blade with a locking tab; the engine harness ground eyelet also lands on the upper bolt stud
Common access obstacle: the downpipe heat shield blocks the lower bolt on some trims—remove its two 10-mm bolts (Service Manual Section 4-5) and swing the shield aside

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2000 Honda Civic?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2000 Honda Civic is on the front of the engine, passenger-side lower, directly below the exhaust manifold and just above the oil filter. It is easily reachable from above once you remove the downpipe heat shield if it is in the way.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2001 Nissan Pathfinder.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2001 Nissan Pathfinder (R50) with the 3.5L VQ35DE V6 mounts the starter on the passenger-side lower rear of the engine, against the transmission bellhousing. A front skid plate on 4WD models and the right-side exhaust manifold heat shield are the main obstacles.
bullets: Exact location: passenger-side lower rear, bolted to the transmission bellhousing above the right front axle shaft
Mounting bolt torque spec: 32–40 ft-lb (43–54 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bellhousing bolts
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and wait 3 minutes; the B+ cable at the solenoid is always live
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable is secured with a 13-mm nut; S-terminal uses a push-on blade with a locking tab; a ground strap bracket clips onto the upper bolt boss
Common access obstacle: on 4WD models, remove the front skid plate (four 12-mm bolts) and lower the exhaust Y-pipe heat shield (three 10-mm bolts) to gain wrench clearance (FSM Section EM)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2001 Nissan Pathfinder?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2001 Nissan Pathfinder is on the passenger-side lower rear of the engine, bolted to the transmission bellhousing above the right front axle shaft. On 4WD models, remove the front skid plate and exhaust heat shield to reach the mounting bolts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2003 Honda Pilot.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2003 Honda Pilot (1st generation) with the 3.5L J35A4 V6 places the starter on the front of the engine, passenger side, beneath the intake manifold. An intake manifold support bracket and wiring tray must be moved before you can see the upper mounting bolt.
bullets: Exact location: front of engine, passenger-side lower, below the intake manifold runners and behind the power steering pump bracket
Mounting bolt torque spec: 32 ft-lb (44 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bolts; the upper bolt is a long through-bolt, the lower is short
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal first and wait 3 minutes for the PCM to power down before removing the B+ cable nut
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 12-mm nut; S-terminal is a push-on blade with a locking tab; the engine harness is clipped to a bracket on the starter body with a plastic P-clip
Common access obstacle: the intake manifold support brace and the wiring tray block the upper bolt—remove the brace’s two 12-mm bolts and swing the tray toward the radiator (Service Manual page 4-9)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2003 Honda Pilot?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2003 Honda Pilot is on the front of the engine, passenger-side lower, below the intake manifold and behind the power steering pump bracket. Remove the intake manifold support brace and wiring tray to reach the upper mounting bolt.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2001 Ford Explorer.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: On the 2001 Ford Explorer (U250) with the 4.0L SOHC V6 or 5.0L V8, the starter is mounted on the passenger-side lower engine block at the transmission bellhousing. The right-side frame rail and catalytic converter are the tight spots.
bullets: Exact location: passenger-side (RH) lower engine block, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just behind the catalytic converter flange
Mounting bolt torque spec: 18 lb-ft (25 N·m) for the two M10 starter bolts; do not exceed 22 lb-ft to avoid thread damage in the aluminum transmission case
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and wait 1 minute; the B+ cable nut at the solenoid is live until removed
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 13-mm nut on the integrated solenoid; the S-terminal signal wire uses a push-lock connector with a red locking tab
Common access obstacle: the right catalytic converter and its flange studs limit socket swing—remove the converter-to-manifold nuts (15-mm) and lower the converter 2 inches (Workshop Manual 303-06)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2001 Ford Explorer?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2001 Ford Explorer is on the passenger-side lower engine block, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just behind the right-hand catalytic converter. Lowering the catalytic converter 2 inches gives enough room to reach the mounting bolts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2014 Toyota Camry.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2014 Toyota Camry (XV50) with the 2.5L 2AR-FE or 3.5L 2GR-FE mounts the starter on the front of the engine, driver’s side, beneath the intake manifold. On the 2.5L, the engine mount bracket and wiring tray sit directly above it.
bullets: Exact location: front of engine, driver’s-side lower, beneath the intake manifold and behind the engine mount torque rod bracket
Mounting bolt torque spec: 29 ft-lb (39 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 mounting bolts; the long bolt goes through the starter nose, the short bolt threads into the transmission case
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and wait 2 minutes for the hybrid-control capacitors to discharge even on non-hybrid models
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 12-mm nut; S-terminal is a push-on blade with a locking tab; the engine wire harness clamp bracket bolts to the upper starter stud with an 8-mm bolt
Common access obstacle: the engine mount torque rod and wiring tray block the upper bolt—remove the torque rod’s 14-mm nut and bolt, then swing the tray toward the battery (RM Step ST-5)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2014 Toyota Camry?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2014 Toyota Camry is on the front of the engine, driver’s-side lower, beneath the intake manifold and behind the engine mount torque rod bracket. Remove the torque rod and swing the wiring tray aside to reach the two mounting bolts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2005 Nissan Sentra.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2005 Nissan Sentra (B15) with the 1.8L QG18DE or 2.5L QR25DE carries the starter on the driver’s-side front lower engine, bolted to the transmission bellhousing. On the 2.5L SE-R Spec V, the alternator bracket overlaps the upper bolt.
bullets: Exact location: driver’s-side front lower, bolted to the transmission bellhousing (1.8L) or left-rear of block (2.5L QR25DE)
Mounting bolt torque spec: 29–36 ft-lb (39–49 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bellhousing bolts
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal first; the B+ stud at the solenoid remains live until the 12-mm nut is removed
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 12-mm nut; S-terminal is a push-on blade with a plastic locking tab; a small P-clip grounds the shield braid to the upper bolt boss
Common access obstacle: on QR25DE models, the alternator bracket blocks the upper bolt—loosen the alternator’s two 14-mm bolts and swing it forward 2 inches (FSM Section EM)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2005 Nissan Sentra?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2005 Nissan Sentra is on the driver’s-side front lower engine, bolted to the transmission bellhousing. On the 2.5L SE-R Spec V, the alternator bracket overlaps the upper bolt, so swing the alternator forward to gain access.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2004 Ford Escape.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2004 Ford Escape with the 3.0L Duratec V6 (most common) places the starter on the front of the engine, passenger side, below the exhaust manifold. The 2.0L Zetec puts it in a similar spot but with more room above.
bullets: Exact location: front of engine, passenger-side lower, below the exhaust manifold and above the oil pan rail on 3.0L Duratec models
Mounting bolt torque spec: 18 lb-ft (25 N·m) for the two M10 starter bolts; do not exceed 20 lb-ft in the aluminum transmission case
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and wait 1 minute; the B+ cable at the solenoid is always live
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 13-mm nut; S-terminal uses a push-lock connector with a red locking tab; the oxygen-sensor harness clip sits on the starter body and must be pried off
Common access obstacle: the exhaust manifold downpipe and its heat shield block the upper bolt—remove the shield’s three 8-mm bolts and lower the pipe 1 inch (Workshop Manual Section 303-06)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2004 Ford Escape?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2004 Ford Escape is on the front of the engine, passenger-side lower, below the exhaust manifold and above the oil pan rail. Remove the exhaust downpipe heat shield and lower the pipe slightly to reach the two mounting bolts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2007 Honda Pilot.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2007 Honda Pilot (1st generation) with the 3.5L J35A6 or J35A9 V6 places the starter on the front of the engine, passenger side, beneath the intake manifold. An intake manifold support bracket and the wiring tray must be moved before you can reach the upper bolt.
bullets: Exact location: front of engine, passenger-side lower, below the intake manifold runners and behind the power steering pump bracket
Mounting bolt torque spec: 32 ft-lb (44 N·m) for the two M10 x 1.25 bolts; the upper is a long through-bolt, the lower is short
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal first and wait 3 minutes for the PCM to power down before removing the B+ cable nut
Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 12-mm nut; S-terminal is a push-on blade with a locking tab; the engine harness is clipped to a bracket on the starter body with a plastic P-clip
Common access obstacle: the intake manifold support brace and wiring tray block the upper bolt—remove the brace’s two 12-mm bolts and swing the tray toward the radiator (Service Manual page 4-9)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2007 Honda Pilot?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2007 Honda Pilot is on the front of the engine, passenger-side lower, below the intake manifold and behind the power steering pump bracket. Remove the intake manifold support brace and wiring tray to reach the upper mounting bolt.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2021 Jeep Cherokee.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start
supportNote.intro: The 2021 Jeep Cherokee (KL) with the 2.4L Tigershark I4 or 3.2L Pentastar V6 mounts the starter on the passenger-side lower engine block at the transmission bellhousing. On the 3.2L V6, a close-coupled catalytic converter and heat shield sit directly in front of it.
bullets: Exact location: passenger-side (RH) lower engine block, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just behind the close-coupled catalytic converter on 3.2L V6 models
Mounting bolt torque spec: 20 lb-ft (27 N·m) for the two M10 starter bolts; do not exceed 25 lb-ft to avoid stripping the aluminum transmission threads
Battery disconnect requirement: disconnect negative terminal and wait 2 minutes; the B+ cable at the solenoid is always live

Wiring harness clip locations: B+ cable uses a 13-mm nut on the integrated solenoid; the S-terminal signal wire uses a push-lock connector with a white locking tab; a ground strap eyelet also mounts on the upper bolt stud
Common access obstacle: the close-coupled catalytic converter heat shield blocks direct access—remove the shield’s three 10-mm bolts and lower it 1 inch (Factory Service Manual 8W-20)

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 2021 Jeep Cherokee?
faq.answer: The starter on a 2021 Jeep Cherokee is on the passenger-side lower engine block, bolted to the transmission bellhousing just behind the close-coupled catalytic converter. Remove the converter heat shield and lower it slightly to reach the two mounting bolts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact battery group size, cold cranking amps, terminal layout, and replacement steps for the 2001 Ford Escape.

supportNote.title: Use the correct group size battery with the right CCA rating
supportNote.intro: The 2001 Ford Escape uses a Group 40R battery, but engine choice matters for CCA. The V6 3.0L calls for 590 CCA, while the 2.0L inline-4 uses 500 CCA. Both fit the same tray, so verify your engine before buying.
bullets: Group size 40R with 500–590 CCA depending on engine
Positive terminal on the right (standard top-post layout)
Hold-down bracket torque: 8–10 ft-lb on the J-bolt nut
No BMS or ECU reset required after replacement
Avoid Group 96R batteries — they are for hybrid models only

faq.question: What size battery does a 2001 Ford Escape take?
faq.answer: A 2001 Ford Escape takes a Group 40R battery. The 3.0L V6 requires 590 CCA, while the 2.0L inline-4 uses 500 CCA. Both use standard top-post terminals with the positive on the right.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact battery group size, cold cranking amps, terminal layout, and replacement steps for the 2004 Acura TSX.

supportNote.title: Use the correct group size battery with the right CCA rating
supportNote.intro: The 2004 Acura TSX uses a compact Group 51R battery with reverse terminal layout. Because the positive post is on the right side (reverse of many domestic batteries), installing a standard-terminal battery will stretch the cables and risk poor connections.
bullets: Group size 51R with 500 CCA minimum
Reverse terminal layout — positive post on the right
Hold-down bracket torque: 8–10 ft-lb on the wedge bolt
No BMS or ECU reset required after replacement
Do not use Group 51 (non-R) — terminal orientation is wrong

faq.question: What size battery does a 2004 Acura TSX take?
faq.answer: A 2004 Acura TSX takes a Group 51R battery with 500 CCA. It uses a reverse-terminal layout, meaning the positive terminal is on the right side when facing the battery.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact battery group size, cold cranking amps, terminal layout, and replacement steps for the 2008 Cadillac CTS.

supportNote.title: Use the correct group size battery with the right CCA rating
supportNote.intro: The 2008 Cadillac CTS hides its battery in the trunk on the right side, not the engine bay. Because it lives inside the cabin, the battery must be sealed and have a vent tube provision. After replacement, the electronic throttle control needs a relearn procedure or the engine may idle poorly.
bullets: Group size 48 / H6 with 730 CCA minimum
Positive terminal on the right (standard layout for H6)
Hold-down bracket torque: 10–12 ft-lb on the strap bolts
Throttle relearn required: ignition ON 3 minutes, OFF, then start and idle 30 seconds
Always connect the vent tube — hydrogen gas must exit the vehicle

faq.question: What size battery does a 2008 Cadillac CTS take?
faq.answer: A 2008 Cadillac CTS takes a Group 48 / H6 battery with 730 CCA. The battery is located in the trunk on the passenger side and must have a vent tube connection to route gases outside the vehicle.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact battery group size, cold cranking amps, terminal layout, and replacement steps for the 2009 Honda Fit.

supportNote.title: Use the correct group size battery with the right CCA rating
supportNote.intro: The 2009 Honda Fit uses one of the smallest automotive batteries on the market — the Group 151R. Its compact size is necessary to fit the tight engine bay, but do not substitute a larger battery because the tray and hold-down will not accommodate it.
bullets: Group size 151R with 335–410 CCA
Positive terminal on the right (standard top-post)
Hold-down bracket torque: 6–8 ft-lb on the wedge bolt
No BMS or ECU reset required after replacement
Do not substitute Group 51R — it is too tall for the hood clearance

faq.question: What size battery does a 2009 Honda Fit take?
faq.answer: A 2009 Honda Fit takes a Group 151R battery with 335–410 CCA. It is a compact battery designed specifically for the Fit's tight engine compartment.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact battery group size, cold cranking amps, terminal layout, and replacement steps for the 2013 Volkswagen Beetle.
extraKeywords: vw beetle battery location engine bay

supportNote.title: Use the correct group size battery with the right CCA rating
supportNote.intro: The 2013 Volkswagen Beetle's battery size depends on the engine. The 2.5L gas engine uses a Group 48 / H6 battery with 760 CCA, while the 2.0L TDI uses a smaller Group H5 / 47 with 650 CCA. Both are located in the engine compartment on the passenger side.

supportNote.title: Use the correct bulb type and never touch the glass
