profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2006 Nissan Pathfinder.

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The 2006 Pathfinder with the 4.0L V6 VQ40DE uses a long 6-rib serpentine belt that wraps around seven pulleys. Routing mistakes around the idler pulleys are common and can shred a new belt in minutes.
bullets: Belt length / part number: 98.0 in (Gates K060980) or OEM 11720-EA200
Number of ribs: 6-rib
Tensioner release direction: Clockwise
Accessory pulley layout order: Crank → Water pump → Tensioner → Power steering → Alternator → Idler → A/C compressor → Crank
Common routing mistake: Missing the flat idler pulley between the alternator and A/C compressor, which causes belt misalignment and squeal

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2006 Nissan Pathfinder take?
faq.answer: The 2006 Nissan Pathfinder with the 4.0L V6 takes a 6-rib, 98.0-inch serpentine belt. Common aftermarket part numbers are Gates K060980 and Dayco 5060980; the Nissan OEM part number is 11720-EA200.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2007 Nissan Pathfinder.

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The 2007 Pathfinder 4.0L V6 VQ40DE retains the same long 6-rib serpentine layout as the 2005–2006 models. Double-check idler pulley placement during installation to avoid belt walk-off.
bullets: Belt length / part number: 98.0 in (Gates K060980) or OEM 11720-EA200
Number of ribs: 6-rib
Tensioner release direction: Clockwise
Accessory pulley layout order: Crank → Water pump → Tensioner → Power steering → Alternator → Idler → A/C compressor → Crank
Common routing mistake: Reversing the alternator and idler path, which removes tension from the power-steering pulley and causes immediate belt slippage

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2007 Nissan Pathfinder take?
faq.answer: The 2007 Nissan Pathfinder with the 4.0L V6 uses a 6-rib, 98.0-inch serpentine belt. Aftermarket equivalents include Gates K060980 and Dayco 5060980; the Nissan OEM part number is 11720-EA200.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2013 Nissan Rogue.

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The 2013 Rogue with the 2.5L QR25DE uses a compact 6-rib serpentine belt. Because the engine bay is tight, access the tensioner from above with a long 14 mm wrench or serpentine tool.
bullets: Belt length / part number: 52.5 in (Gates K060525) or OEM 11720-1VA0A
Number of ribs: 6-rib
Tensioner release direction: Clockwise
Accessory pulley layout order: Crank → Tensioner → Alternator → Idler → A/C compressor → Water pump → Crank
Common routing mistake: Failing to seat the belt fully on the lower crank pulley before releasing tensioner, which causes the belt to ride 1 rib off and squeal

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2013 Nissan Rogue take?
faq.answer: The 2013 Nissan Rogue with the 2.5L 4-cylinder takes a 6-rib, 52.5-inch serpentine belt. Common aftermarket replacements are Gates K060525 and Dayco 5060525; the Nissan OEM part number is 11720-1VA0A.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2007 Honda Fit.
extraKeywords: honda fit ac belt part number

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The 2007 Honda Fit 1.5L L15A1 does not use a single serpentine belt; it uses two separate multi-ribbed belts with manual tensioners. Replace both at the same service interval to avoid a roadside failure.
bullets: Belt length / part number: Alternator belt 44.5 in / 5PK1130 (OEM 31110-PWA-004); A/C belt 35.0 in / 4PK888 (OEM 38920-PWA-004)
Number of ribs: 5-rib (alternator), 4-rib (A/C)
Tensioner release direction: Manual adjustment via alternator pivot bolt (12 mm) and lock bolt; A/C idler uses 14 mm adjustment bolt
Accessory pulley layout order: Alternator belt — Crank → Alternator; A/C belt — Crank → A/C compressor (via idler)
Common routing mistake: Overtightening the alternator belt past 10 mm deflection at 10 lbf, which destroys the crankshaft bearings and alternator bearings prematurely

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2007 Honda Fit take?
faq.answer: The 2007 Honda Fit 1.5L uses two separate drive belts, not one serpentine. The alternator belt is a 5-rib, 44.5-inch belt (5PK1130 / OEM 31110-PWA-004), and the A/C compressor belt is a 4-rib, 35.0-inch belt (4PK888 / OEM 38920-PWA-004).

Number of ribs: 6-rib (both engines)

Accessory pulley layout order (3.4L): Crank → Tensioner → PS pump → Alternator → Water pump → Idler → A/C compressor → Crank
Common routing mistake: Installing the belt under the water-pump idler instead of over it, which reverses pump rotation and causes immediate overheating

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2003 Chevrolet Impala take?
faq.answer: The base 2003 Chevrolet Impala with the 3.4L V6 takes a 6-rib, 80.3-inch serpentine belt (Gates K060802 / Dayco 5060802 / OEM 12590767). If equipped with the 3.8L V6, use a 6-rib, 82.5-inch belt (Gates K060825).

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2005 Nissan Pathfinder.

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The first-year R51 2005 Pathfinder introduced the 4.0L V6 VQ40DE and its long 6-rib serpentine drive. The belt path is identical to 2006–2010 models, so the same routing diagram and part numbers apply.
bullets: Belt length / part number: 98.0 in (Gates K060980) or OEM 11720-EA200
Number of ribs: 6-rib
Tensioner release direction: Clockwise
Accessory pulley layout order: Crank → Water pump → Tensioner → Power steering → Alternator → Idler → A/C compressor → Crank
Common routing mistake: Routing the belt behind the smooth tensioner pulley instead of in front of it, which costs roughly 4 inches of effective length and prevents proper tensioning

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2005 Nissan Pathfinder take?
faq.answer: The 2005 Nissan Pathfinder with the 4.0L V6 uses a 6-rib, 98.0-inch serpentine belt. Aftermarket equivalents include Gates K060980 and Dayco 5060980; the Nissan OEM part number is 11720-EA200.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2010 Nissan Pathfinder.

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The 2010 Pathfinder carries over the 4.0L V6 VQ40DE and its 98-inch 6-rib serpentine belt. A worn tensioner pulley bearing is the leading cause of repeated belt squeal on high-mileage examples.
bullets: Belt length / part number: 98.0 in (Gates K060980) or OEM 11720-EA200
Number of ribs: 6-rib
Tensioner release direction: Clockwise
Accessory pulley layout order: Crank → Water pump → Tensioner → Power steering → Alternator → Idler → A/C compressor → Crank
Common routing mistake: Ignoring a seized idler pulley that feels gritty when spun by hand; it will destroy the new belt within 500 miles

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2010 Nissan Pathfinder take?
faq.answer: The 2010 Nissan Pathfinder with the 4.0L V6 takes a 6-rib, 98.0-inch serpentine belt. Common replacements are Gates K060980 and Dayco 5060980; the Nissan OEM part number is 11720-EA200.

Number of ribs: 6-rib (both engines)

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2010 Nissan Altima take?
faq.answer: The 2010 Nissan Altima with the 2.5L 4-cylinder takes a 6-rib, 52.5-inch serpentine belt (Gates K060525 / OEM 11720-JA00B). If equipped with the 3.5L V6, the belt is a 6-rib, 82.5-inch belt (Gates K060825).

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2012 Honda Accord take?

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2012 Nissan Rogue take?

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact thermostat housing location, temperature rating, bolt torque, and coolant bleed sequence for the 2012 Nissan Sentra.

supportNote.title: Use the correct temperature rating and torque the housing evenly
supportNote.intro: On the 2012 Nissan Sentra with the 2.0L MR20DE engine, the thermostat is mounted in the lower water inlet housing at the front of the engine block. A stuck thermostat is the most common cause of overheating or slow warm-up on this engine.
bullets: Thermostat location: lower water inlet housing, front of engine block, below the intake manifold
Temperature rating: 170°F / 76°C (OEM spec)
Housing bolt torque spec: 7.4 ft-lb (10 Nm)
Coolant type: Nissan Blue Long Life Antifreeze/Coolant (OAT); capacity approximately 7.1 qt (6.7 L)
Bleed procedure: fill radiator and reservoir, install cap, start engine and run with heater on full hot at 2,000 RPM for 2–3 minutes, then top off when cool

faq.question: 2012 nissan sentra thermostat location
faq.answer: The thermostat on a 2012 Nissan Sentra (2.0L MR20DE) sits in the lower water inlet housing at the front of the engine block, below the intake manifold where the lower radiator hose attaches. The OEM thermostat opens at 170°F (76°C), and the two 10 mm housing bolts torque to 7.4 ft-lb (10 Nm).

supportNote.title: Use the correct temperature rating and torque the housing evenly
supportNote.intro: On the 2003 Ford F-150 with the 4.6L or 5.4L Triton V8, the thermostat sits in the upper housing at the front of the intake manifold. A P0128 code or slow warm-up in cold weather is a classic sign of a stuck-open thermostat on these engines.
bullets: Thermostat location: upper housing at the front of the intake manifold, where the upper radiator hose connects
Temperature rating: 192°F / 89°C (OEM spec)
Housing bolt torque spec: 18 Nm (13 ft-lb)

Bleed procedure: fill the degas bottle to the cold fill line, run engine with the pressure cap loose until the thermostat opens, squeeze the upper hose to purge air, then top off and tighten the cap

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact starter motor location, bolt torque, wiring checks, and step-by-step removal for the 2003 Hyundai Elantra.

supportNote.title: Know the starter location and bolt torque before you start

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact oil weight, capacity with filter, drain plug torque, and reset procedure for the 2014 Ford Fusion.

supportNote.title: Use the factory oil weight and replace the crush washer
supportNote.intro: The 2014 Fusion's EcoBoost and Duratec engines are sensitive to oil viscosity. Using the wrong weight can affect VVT operation and fuel economy. Always verify engine code before purchasing oil.
bullets: Oil weight and API spec: 5W-20 API SN (2.5L) or 5W-30 API SN (2.0L EcoBoost)

Maintenance light reset: Scroll to Oil Life on instrument cluster, press and hold OK until 100%

faq.question: What oil does a 2014 Ford Fusion take?
faq.answer: The 2014 Ford Fusion takes SAE 5W-20 in the 2.5L four-cylinder and SAE 5W-30 in the 2.0L EcoBoost turbo. Capacity is 4.5 quarts with filter for the 2.0L and 5.7 quarts with filter for the 2.5L. Use API SN or newer spec synthetic blend or full synthetic oil.

faq.answer: The 2008 Saturn Vue takes SAE 5W-30 in the 2.4L four-cylinder and 3.6L V6, and SAE 5W-20 in the 3.5L V6. Capacity ranges from 3.8 to 5.5 quarts with filter depending on engine. The 3.6L V6 requires 5.5 quarts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact oil weight, capacity with filter, drain plug torque, and reset procedure for the 2006 Volkswagen Beetle.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact NGK or Denso plug numbers, gap specification, and torque for the 2012 Subaru Forester.
extraKeywords: Subaru boxer spark plug socket size

supportNote.title: Gap and torque are the two most common mistakes
supportNote.intro: The 2012 Forester with the FB25 2.5L boxer engine uses a 12mm-thread iridium plug that is easy to gap incorrectly or overtighten. Because the plugs sit deep in the side-mounted valve covers, access is awkward and a torque wrench is strongly recommended.
bullets: OEM plug: NGK SILZKAR7B11 (stock #93482) Laser Iridium — fine-wire iridium center electrode with platinum-tipped ground electrode
Gap specification: 0.044 in (1.1 mm) — do not gap iridium electrodes by prying against the center tip
Torque spec for aluminum head: 15.2 ft-lb (21 N·m) per Subaru; NGK rates 10.8–14.5 ft-lb on this 12mm gasket-seat plug
Coil-on-plug ignition — remove the ignition coil bolt (10 mm) and pull the coil straight up; a 14 mm (9/16 in) thin-wall spark plug socket with a short extension is required
Anti-seize: use a very thin film on threads if desired, then reduce torque by roughly one-third; apply dielectric grease inside the coil boot before reinstallation

faq.question: What spark plugs does a 2012 Subaru Forester take?
faq.answer: The 2012 Subaru Forester 2.5L uses NGK SILZKAR7B11 (stock #93482) Laser Iridium plugs. The gap is 0.044 in (1.1 mm) and the aluminum-head torque is 15.2 ft-lb (21 N·m). Denso IXEH22TT (Iridium TT) is also a compatible replacement. You need four plugs and a 14 mm thin-wall socket.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact NGK or Denso plug numbers, gap specification, and torque for the 2013 Honda Fit.
extraKeywords: Honda Fit coil on plug socket size

supportNote.title: Gap and torque are the two most common mistakes
supportNote.intro: The 2013 Fit 1.5L uses a long-reach iridium plug that is factory-set but should still be verified. Because the head is aluminum and the plug threads are fine, overtightening strips threads easily.
bullets: OEM plug: Denso SKJ20DR-M13 (Honda PN 12290-RB1-004) Iridium Long Life — NGK ZFR6F-11 or NGK LKAR7BIX-11S are common aftermarket equivalents
Gap specification: 0.039–0.043 in (1.0–1.1 mm); verify with a wire feeler gauge because the iridium tip is fragile
Torque spec for aluminum head: 13 ft-lb (18 N·m) — if using anti-seize, reduce torque to about 9 ft-lb
Coil-on-plug ignition — remove the 10 mm coil bolt, pull the coil, and use a 14 mm thin-wall spark plug socket with a short extension
Anti-seize and dielectric grease: a very small dab of nickel anti-seize on threads only; coat the inside of the coil boot with dielectric grease to prevent moisture corrosion

faq.question: What spark plugs does a 2013 Honda Fit take?
faq.answer: The 2013 Honda Fit 1.5L uses Denso SKJ20DR-M13 (Honda PN 12290-RB1-004) iridium long-life plugs. The gap is 1.0–1.1 mm (0.039–0.043 in) and torque is 13 ft-lb (18 N·m) into the aluminum head. NGK ZFR6F-11 or LKAR7BIX-11S are quality alternatives. You need four plugs.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact NGK or Denso plug numbers, gap specification, and torque for the 2012 Honda Odyssey.
extraKeywords: Honda Odyssey 3.5L V6 coil on plug

supportNote.title: Gap and torque are the two most common mistakes
supportNote.intro: The 2012 Odyssey 3.5L V6 uses a laser-iridium plug with a narrow gap. The rear bank is under the intake manifold cover, so patience and a proper socket setup are required. This engine is sensitive to incorrect gap and overtightening.
bullets: OEM plug: NGK DILZKR7A11G (stock #92924) Laser Iridium — Honda PN 12290-R71-L01; Denso DXU22HCR-D11S is an equivalent replacement
Gap specification: 0.044 in (1.1 mm) — plugs are factory pre-gapped; do not adjust iridium electrodes by prying
Torque spec for aluminum head: 16 ft-lb (22 N·m) per Honda service manual; tighten 2/3 turn after the gasket contacts the head if no torque wrench is available
Coil-on-plug ignition with six individual coils — remove the intake manifold cover and ignition coil cover to access all plugs; a 14 mm thin-wall socket with multiple extensions is needed
Anti-seize and dielectric grease: apply a small amount of anti-seize to plug threads (reduce torque by ~30% if used); apply dielectric grease inside each coil boot

faq.question: What spark plugs does a 2012 Honda Odyssey take?

faq.question: What spark plugs does a 2010 Nissan Altima take?

supportNote.intro: The 2019 Civic 1.5T and 2.0L engines use a 12mm-thread laser-iridium plug with a very narrow gap. The 1.5T is especially sensitive to incorrect gap, which causes misfires under boost. A special thin-wall 14 mm (or 12mm-specific) socket is required.

profile.titleSuffix: Water Pump Location & Timing Belt Note
profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact water pump location, gasket vs O-ring, timing belt relation, and torque specs for the 2015 Ford Explorer.
extraKeywords: Ford Explorer water pump behind timing cover
Explorer water pump coolant in oil

supportNote.title: Check if the pump is behind the timing cover before ordering parts
supportNote.intro: On the 2015 Ford Explorer with the 3.5L V6, the water pump is driven by the timing chain and mounted inside the engine. If coolant is disappearing with no visible leak, the pump may be leaking into the crankcase.
bullets: Exact location: Inside the engine, driven by the front timing chain (3.5L V6); external belt-driven on the 2.0L EcoBoost
Gasket or O-ring type: Sealed housing with internal O-ring on the 3.5L pump module
Torque spec for mounting bolts: Timing cover bolts 18 lb-ft; pump housing bolts 89 lb-in
Timing belt or chain relation: Driven by the primary timing chain on the 3.5L V6; entire timing cover must be removed
What else to replace while in there: Timing chains, guides, tensioners, front crank seal, and coolant

faq.question: Where is the water pump on a 2015 Ford Explorer?
faq.answer: On the 3.5L V6, the water pump is mounted inside the engine and driven by the timing chain, located behind the front timing cover. On the 2.0L EcoBoost, it is external and driven by the serpentine belt at the front of the engine.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2005 Kia Sportage.

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The 2005 Sportage uses different belt configurations depending on engine and A/C equipment. The 2.7L V6 is the more common powertrain and routes a longer main belt around the alternator, power steering, and crank pulleys, while some configurations use a separate A/C belt. Always verify underhood sticker routing before removal.
bullets: Belt length / part number: ~79.0 in. (2005 mm), 6-rib; OEM 25212-23721 or aftermarket 6PK2005
Number of ribs: 6
Tensioner release direction: Rotate automatic tensioner arm clockwise to release spring pressure
Accessory pulley layout order: Crankshaft → alternator → power steering pump → tensioner idler (A/C on separate belt where equipped)
Common routing mistake or idler pulley note: Models with and without A/C use different belt lengths; do not interchange — verify rib seating on the smooth tensioner pulley

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2005 Kia Sportage take?
faq.answer: The 2005 Kia Sportage with the 2.7L V6 uses a 6-rib serpentine belt approximately 79.0 inches (2005 mm) in effective length. OEM part number is 25212-23721. The 2.0L I4 uses a shorter belt; always confirm your engine code before ordering.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2018 Ford Transit.

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The 2018 Transit has engine-specific belt setups: the 3.5L EcoBoost uses two separate serpentine belts (A and B), while the 3.7L V6 and 3.2L diesel use a single belt. Never assume a one-belt layout until you verify under the hood.
bullets: Belt length / part number: 3.5L EcoBoost — two belts (A/C belt + water pump/alternator belt), each ~6-rib; 3.7L V6 — single belt ~55.2 in. (1404 mm), 6-rib; OEM BL3Z-8620 series
Number of ribs: 6 (all engines)
Tensioner release direction: Rotate the automatic tensioner arm clockwise with a 15 mm socket or breaker bar
Accessory pulley layout order: 3.5L EcoBoost Belt A: crankshaft → A/C compressor → idler; Belt B: crankshaft → water pump → alternator → idler
Common routing mistake or idler pulley note: EcoBoost owners often miss the second smaller belt; replace both belts together and inspect both idler pulleys for wobble

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2018 Ford Transit take?
faq.answer: It depends on the engine. The 3.7L V6 uses a single 6-rib belt approximately 55.2 inches (1404 mm) long. The 3.5L EcoBoost uses two separate 6-rib belts: one for the A/C compressor and one for the water pump and alternator. The 3.2L diesel uses its own unique belt length.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact belt length, routing diagram, tensioner direction, and pulley layout for the 2018 Mitsubishi Outlander.

supportNote.title: Get the routing diagram and belt length before starting
supportNote.intro: The 2018 Outlander's 2.4L inline-4 uses a single 6-rib belt with an automatic spring-tensioner mounted low on the passenger side. The 3.0L V6 GT uses a longer belt and different routing, so engine verification is critical before ordering.
bullets: Belt length / part number: 2.4L — ~86.5 in. (2197 mm), 6-rib; OEM 1340A150 or Dayco 5060865
Number of ribs: 6
Tensioner release direction: Rotate the automatic tensioner clockwise with a 14 mm wrench or socket to release tension
Accessory pulley layout order: Crankshaft → A/C compressor → alternator → tensioner → power steering pump → idler

Common routing mistake or idler pulley note: The smooth tensioner pulley and the ribbed idler pulley are positioned close together; double-check that the ribbed side of the belt contacts the ribbed idler and the smooth back rides the smooth tensioner

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 2018 Mitsubishi Outlander take?
faq.answer: The 2018 Mitsubishi Outlander with the 2.4L inline-4 engine uses a 6-rib serpentine belt approximately 86.5 inches (2197 mm) long. OEM part number is 1340A150. The 3.0L V6 GT uses a longer belt with a different routing pattern.

bullets: Belt length / part number: 2.4L — ~86.5 in. (2197 mm), 6-rib; OEM 1340A150 or Dayco 5060865

Assembly vs bulb only: Twist socket to remove; replace full assembly if housing or lens is damaged

Bulb number: 7443 tail/stop, 7440A amber turn, 921 reverse (sedan)

Pulley layout: Crank -> A/C compressor -> Idler -> Alternator -> Tensioner -> Power steering -> Water pump (verify with routing sticker)
Common mistake: Routing belt under the idler pulley instead of over it, or failing to fully seat the belt in the deep crank pulley grooves

O-ring lubricant: Clean engine oil or assembly lube on new tank seal O-ring before seating module

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact H7, D1S, and H9 bulb fitment for the 2008 BMW X5 E70 by trim and headlight type.

supportNote.title: 2008 BMW X5 headlight jobs fail when you guess the bulb
supportNote.intro: The E70 X5 shipped with three headlight configurations: halogen, xenon adaptive, and xenon non-adaptive. Ordering the wrong bulb type is the #1 mistake.
bullets: Halogen trims: Low beam = H7, high beam = H7 or H9. Check the back of the existing bulb before ordering.
Xenon trims: Low/high beam = D1S 35W HID. Do not touch the glass with bare fingers — skin oils cause hot spots and early failure.
Access: Turn the wheel inward, remove the fender liner fasteners, and reach the bulb cover from the wheel well. The engine bay is too tight for direct access.
Xenon igniters are integrated into the bulb base on factory D1S units. Aftermarket D1S may have a separate igniter — verify wiring length.
Re-aim headlights after bulb replacement if you notice uneven cutoff patterns on a flat wall.

faq.question: What headlight bulb does a 2008 BMW X5 take?
faq.answer: It depends on trim. Halogen-equipped 2008 BMW X5 models use H7 bulbs for low and high beam. Xenon-equipped models use D1S 35W HID bulbs. Check your existing bulb, owner manual, or the under-hood label to confirm before buying.

profile.descriptionSuffix: E70 X5 serpentine belt replacement for N52 inline-6 and N62 V8 engines with routing diagrams.

supportNote.title: 2008 BMW X5 belt routing changes completely between the N52 and N62
supportNote.intro: The E70 X5 came with two very different engines. The N52 inline-6 uses one long serpentine belt. The N62 V8 uses two shorter belts. Mixing up the routing or belt length destroys the repair.
bullets: N52 3.0L inline-6: One 6-rib serpentine belt (~1,700 mm) drives alternator, A/C, power steering, and water pump. Tensioner is spring-loaded automatic.
N62 4.8L V8: Two belts — a main serpentine (~1,530 mm) and a secondary A/C belt (~940 mm). Both have automatic tensioners.
Before removing anything, photograph the routing or check the under-hood sticker. N62 routing is especially complex around the tensioner and idler pulleys.
Replace the tensioner and idler pulleys if they rattle, wobble, or feel gritty when spun by hand. The N62 idler is a common failure point.
Belt part numbers: N52 = Continental 6PK1700 or equivalent; N62 main = 6PK1530, N62 A/C = 4PK940. Always match rib count and length exactly.

faq.question: How many serpentine belts does a 2008 BMW X5 have?
faq.answer: It depends on the engine. The N52 3.0L inline-6 has one serpentine belt. The N62 4.8L V8 has two belts — a main serpentine belt and a separate A/C compressor belt. Verify your engine code before ordering parts.

profile.descriptionSuffix: Exact air filter location, part numbers, and replacement steps for the 2014 Jeep Grand Cherokee WK2.

supportNote.title: 2014 Grand Cherokee air filters are a 5-minute swap once you know the clip trick
supportNote.intro: The WK2 Grand Cherokee engine air filter sits in a large rectangular box on the driver side of the engine bay. The factory clips are stiff, and the box lid can feel stuck if you try to pry it wrong.
bullets: Open the hood and locate the air filter box on the driver side rear of the engine bay. It has four metal spring clips — two on the front, two on the rear.
Release all four clips, then lift the lid straight up. Do not pry sideways — the lid has alignment tabs that snap off easily.
Remove the old filter and inspect the bottom of the box for debris, leaves, or dust. Vacuum it out if needed.
Install the new filter with the pleats facing down and the rubber seal facing up toward the lid. The filter should sit flush in the tray without gaps.
Factory filter: Mopar 4891695AA or equivalent. Aftermarket options: Fram CA11114, Bosch S5575, K&N 33-2457 (washable).

faq.question: Where is the engine air filter on a 2014 Jeep Grand Cherokee?
faq.answer: The engine air filter is in a rectangular plastic box on the driver side of the engine bay, near the firewall. Release four metal spring clips, lift the lid, and swap the filter. No tools are required.

profile.descriptionSuffix: 7th-gen Ford F-150 radiator replacement for 300 I6 and V8 engines with automatic transmission cooler notes.

supportNote.title: 1985 Ford F-150 radiator jobs are about the transmission cooler lines first
supportNote.intro: The 7th-gen F-150 (1980-1986) uses a simple brass-and-copper radiator, but automatic transmission models have steel cooler lines that rust-seize to the fittings. Breaking a line turns a 2-hour job into a 2-day job.
bullets: Automatic transmission models: The two 3/8-inch steel cooler lines connect to the passenger side of the radiator with inverted-flare fittings. Spray penetrant and let it sit before attempting removal.
Engine options matter: The 300 cubic inch inline-6 and 302/351W V8 use the same core radiator, but the 460 big-block uses a thicker core. Verify before ordering.
Drain the coolant first into a clean pan. Old ethylene glycol is toxic to pets and illegal to dump in most areas — take it to an auto parts store for recycling.
Remove the fan shroud (four bolts) before pulling the radiator. The shroud traps the radiator and will crack if you try to remove both together.
Replace the radiator hoses and thermostat while the system is open. A 35-year-old upper hose will fail soon after you disturb it.

faq.question: How do you replace the radiator on a 1985 Ford F-150?
faq.answer: Drain the coolant, disconnect the transmission cooler lines (auto trans only), remove the upper and lower hoses, detach the fan shroud, and lift the radiator straight up. Replace hoses and thermostat at the same time. On automatic models, be careful not to twist or break the steel cooler lines at the radiator fittings.

profile.descriptionSuffix: F25 BMW X3 battery replacement guide including AGM requirements and battery registration coding steps.

supportNote.title: 2014 BMW X3 batteries live in the trunk and demand coding afterward
supportNote.intro: The F25 X3 (2011-2017) places its 12V battery under the cargo floor panel on the right side. Skipping battery registration after replacement causes premature failure because the charging system never adapts to the new battery capacity.
bullets: Location: Lift the cargo floor panel on the right side. The battery sits in a plastic tray under a vent tube. Disconnect the vent tube before removing the battery.
Battery type: AGM is required (not standard lead-acid). Common sizes are H7 or H8 depending on options. Verify the old battery label for exact DIN size and capacity (typically 80-95 Ah).

Registration is mandatory: Use a BMW-capable scan tool (ISTA, Carly, BimmerCode, or Foxwell NT510 Elite) to register the new battery via the ECU. This tells the charging system the new capacity and type.

Use a memory saver during swap: The F25 stores window positions, seat memory, and radio presets in volatile memory. A 12V memory saver plugged into the OBD2 port or cigarette lighter preserves settings.
Torque the hold-down bracket to 15 N·m. A loose battery can crack the tray or damage the vent system.

faq.question: Does a 2014 BMW X3 battery need to be coded?
faq.answer: Yes. The 2014 BMW X3 (F25) requires battery registration/coding after replacement. The charging management system stores battery capacity and type data. Without registration, the alternator may overcharge or undercharge the new battery, leading to premature failure. Use a BMW-compatible scan tool to complete registration.

profile.descriptionSuffix: C/K 1500 serpentine belt replacement for 5.7L V8 and other engines with routing and tensioner notes.

supportNote.title: 1995 Chevy 1500 belt jobs are won by the tensioner direction
supportNote.intro: The GMT400 C/K 1500 uses a single serpentine belt with an automatic spring tensioner. The tensioner rotates clockwise to release tension. Trying to force it the wrong way snaps the tensioner arm.
bullets: Use a 3/8-inch drive ratchet or breaker bar inserted into the tensioner square drive. Rotate the tensioner CLOCKWISE to release belt tension. Do not use an open-end wrench on the tensioner bolt — it will round off.
5.7L V8 routing: Crank → water pump → power steering → alternator → idler → A/C compressor → tensioner. The under-hood sticker shows the exact path.
Inspect all pulleys while the belt is off. Spin the alternator, water pump, idler, and tensioner pulleys by hand. Any grinding, wobble, or roughness means replacement is needed.
The water pump pulley on GMT400 trucks often develops bearing play at 100K+ miles. If it wobbles, replace the water pump with the belt.
Belt size: 6-rib, approximately 85 inches (varies by A/C and power steering configuration). The exact length is printed on the old belt or the under-hood sticker.

faq.question: Which way does the tensioner go on a 1995 Chevy 1500?
faq.answer: The serpentine belt tensioner on a 1995 Chevrolet C/K 1500 rotates CLOCKWISE to release tension. Insert a 3/8-inch drive ratchet into the tensioner square drive and rotate clockwise. The tensioner is spring-loaded — do not force it counter-clockwise or you risk breaking the tensioner arm.

profile.titleSuffix: Alternator Replacement for N62 V8 & M54 I6
profile.descriptionSuffix: E53 BMW X5 alternator replacement guide covering N62 V8 and M54 inline-6 engines with access tips.

supportNote.title: 2006 BMW X5 alternator access ranges from 90 minutes to 6 hours depending on engine
supportNote.intro: The E53 X5 came with the M54 3.0L inline-6 or the N62 4.4L/4.8L V8. The M54 alternator is at the front of the engine and relatively accessible. The N62 alternator is buried under the intake manifold on the passenger side and requires major disassembly.
bullets: M54 3.0L: The alternator sits at the front right of the engine. Remove the air filter box, disconnect the battery, remove the serpentine belt, and unbolt the alternator (three 13 mm bolts). Job time: 1.5-2 hours.

N62 V8: The alternator is under the intake manifold on the passenger side. You must remove the intake manifold, fuel rails, and multiple coolant hoses. This is an advanced DIY job. Job time: 5-8 hours.
Always disconnect the battery negative terminal before starting. The alternator B+ terminal is live and will arc against the chassis if the battery is connected.
Replace the alternator belt and tensioner while the system is open. The N62 has a separate alternator belt that wears at the same rate.
After installation, verify charging voltage at the battery with the engine running: 13.8-14.4V at idle is normal. Below 13.5V means the alternator or voltage regulator is failing.

faq.question: Where is the alternator on a 2006 BMW X5?
faq.answer: It depends on the engine. On the M54 3.0L inline-6, the alternator is at the front right of the engine and is moderately accessible. On the N62 4.4L/4.8L V8, the alternator is under the intake manifold on the passenger side and requires removing the intake manifold — a much more involved job.

profile.titleSuffix: Serpentine Belt Replacement for N55 & N63 Engines
profile.descriptionSuffix: E70 LCI BMW X5 serpentine belt routing and tensioner guide for N55 and N63 twin-turbo engines.

supportNote.title: 2013 BMW X5 belt replacement requires patience in a very tight engine bay
supportNote.intro: The 2013 E70 LCI X5 uses the N55 single-turbo inline-6 or the N63 twin-turbo V8. Both engines have extremely tight engine bays with minimal clearance around the tensioner and idler pulleys.
bullets: N55 3.0L: One main serpentine belt drives the alternator, A/C, water pump, and power steering. The tensioner is a spring-loaded arm with a 16 mm hex head. Use a long wrench for leverage — the spring is stiff.

N63 4.4L V8: Two belts — a main serpentine and an A/C belt. The main belt routes around the crank, both alternators (yes, the N63 has two), water pump, and power steering. Photograph the routing before removal.
The N63 has an electric water pump that runs after shutdown. Disconnect the battery before working near the pump to prevent unexpected startup.
Check the idler pulley bearings while the belt is off. The N55 idler is a common failure point at 80K+ miles and chirps before seizing.
OEM belt part numbers: N55 = 6PK1805; N63 main = 6PK1745; N63 A/C = 6PK1055. Continental and Gates are reliable aftermarket options.

faq.question: Does a 2013 BMW X5 have one or two serpentine belts?
faq.answer: It depends on the engine. The N55 3.0L inline-6 has one serpentine belt. The N63 4.4L twin-turbo V8 has two belts — a main serpentine belt and a separate A/C compressor belt. The N63 also has two alternators, which makes the main belt routing more complex.

profile.descriptionSuffix: 10th-gen Ford F-150 thermostat replacement for 4.6L and 5.4L Triton V8 engines with torque specs.

supportNote.title: 2001 Ford F-150 thermostat swaps are a perfect first cooling system project
supportNote.intro: The 10th-gen F-150 (1997-2003) with the 4.6L or 5.4L Triton V8 has the thermostat housing at the front of the engine, directly behind the upper radiator hose. It is one of the most accessible thermostat jobs in the truck world.
bullets: Location: Follow the upper radiator hose from the radiator to the engine. The hose connects to a black plastic housing bolted to the intake manifold. This is the thermostat housing.
Remove two 10 mm bolts holding the housing, pull the old thermostat, and scrape the old gasket material off both surfaces. Use a plastic scraper — metal gouges the aluminum intake.
The factory thermostat is 195°F (90°C). Do not use a 160°F or 180°F "performance" thermostat — the ECU expects 195°F for fuel trim calculations and OBD2 readiness.
Torque the housing bolts to 18 lb-ft (24 N·m). The plastic housing cracks if over-tightened. Tighten in a crisscross pattern to seat the gasket evenly.

Refill with Ford-spec Gold or Motorcraft Orange coolant (depending on factory fill) mixed 50/50. Bleed the system by running the engine with the heater on high and the radiator cap off until the thermostat opens.

faq.question: Where is the thermostat on a 2001 Ford F-150?
faq.answer: The thermostat is at the front of the engine, inside the housing where the upper radiator hose connects to the intake manifold. Remove the upper hose, unbolt the two 10 mm housing bolts, and pull the old thermostat. It is one of the most accessible thermostat locations on any truck.

profile.descriptionSuffix: 11th-gen Ford F-150 headlight bulb fitment for 4.6L and 5.4L trims with halogen and HID options.

supportNote.title: 2003 Ford F-150 headlight bulbs are 9007 on halogen trims, but HID trucks use D2R
supportNote.intro: The 11th-gen F-150 (2004-2008, but 2003 is the transition year) uses 9007 halogen bulbs on most trims. Lariat and higher trims with factory HID use D2R xenon bulbs. Ordering the wrong type is expensive and frustrating.
bullets: Halogen trims (XL, XLT, most fleets): Low/high beam = 9007. This is a single-filament dual-beam bulb. Install with the flat tab oriented correctly — forcing it breaks the retainer clips.
HID trims (Lariat, King Ranch, Harley-Davidson): Low beam = D2R 35W xenon. Do not touch the glass with bare fingers. The high beam is a separate halogen bulb (H13/9008) in the same housing.

Access: Open the hood, reach behind the headlight housing, and twist the bulb socket counter-clockwise. The driver side is tight against the battery tray — remove the battery hold-down for extra room if needed.
Aftermarket LED "drop-in" replacements for 9007 sockets often cause flickering and error codes on F-150s. If you want LED, use a properly engineered kit with a CANbus decoder.
Replace both bulbs at the same time. When one 9007 burns out, the other is near end of life. Matched brightness prevents uneven light output.

faq.question: What headlight bulb does a 2003 Ford F-150 take?
faq.answer: Most 2003 Ford F-150 trucks use 9007 halogen bulbs for low and high beam. Higher trims with factory HID headlights use D2R 35W xenon bulbs for low beam and H13/9008 halogen for high beam. Check your owner manual or the bulb label inside the headlight housing to confirm.

profile.descriptionSuffix: R52 Nissan Pathfinder thermostat replacement for 3.5L V6 with torque specs and bleeding procedure.

supportNote.title: 2013 Nissan Pathfinder thermostats sit right behind the upper radiator hose
supportNote.intro: The R52 Pathfinder (2013-2020) with the VQ35DE 3.5L V6 has the thermostat housing bolted to the front of the engine, directly behind the upper radiator hose. It is an easy 30-minute job with basic hand tools.
bullets: Location: Follow the upper radiator hose from the radiator to the engine. The hose connects to a black plastic thermostat housing on the front of the intake manifold.
Remove two 10 mm bolts and gently pry the housing loose. The old gasket may stick — use a plastic scraper to clean both surfaces. Do not use a metal blade on the aluminum intake.
Factory thermostat: 170°F (76°C) or 180°F (82°C) depending on climate package. Most US-market Pathfinders use the 180°F unit. The part number is Nissan 21200-31U13 or Stant 48788.
Torque the housing bolts to 8.7 lb-ft (12 N·m). The plastic housing cracks easily if over-tightened. Tighten evenly in a crisscross pattern.

Refill with Nissan Blue Long Life Coolant or equivalent pre-mixed OAT coolant. Bleed the system by running the engine with the heater on high and the radiator cap off until the upper hose is hot and firm.

faq.question: Where is the thermostat on a 2013 Nissan Pathfinder?
faq.answer: The thermostat is at the front of the engine, inside the housing where the upper radiator hose connects to the intake manifold. Remove the upper hose, unbolt the two 10 mm housing bolts, and pull the old thermostat. It is one of the more accessible thermostat locations on a modern V6 SUV.

profile.titleSuffix: Headlight Bulb Type & Xenon Fitment Guide
profile.descriptionSuffix: E90 BMW 330i headlight bulb replacement for halogen and xenon adaptive headlights with access tips.

supportNote.title: 2006 BMW 330i headlights split between H7 halogen and D1S xenon depending on option package
supportNote.intro: The E90 330i (2006-2011) came with three headlight configurations: base halogen, xenon non-adaptive, and xenon adaptive (adaptive headlights have the turning motor). The bulb type and access method differ significantly.
bullets: Halogen base: Low beam = H7, high beam = H7. Access by removing the front wheel liner on the affected side — the engine bay is too tight for direct hand access.
Xenon trims: Low/high beam = D1S 35W HID. The igniter is integrated into the bulb base on factory units. Do not touch the glass — skin oils create hot spots that crack the quartz.
Adaptive xenon only: The headlight assembly has a self-leveling motor and a turning motor. Do not disconnect the leveling sensor arm or the headlights will aim incorrectly and throw a dash warning.
Replace both D1S bulbs at the same time. Color temperature shifts as xenon bulbs age. A new bulb next to a 5-year-old bulb creates a mismatched white/yellow look.
OEM D1S part: Philips 85415 or Osram 66144. Aftermarket D1S from Morimoto or XenonDepot are quality alternatives at lower cost.

faq.question: What headlight bulb does a 2006 BMW 330i take?
faq.answer: It depends on the headlight option package. Base 330i models use H7 halogen bulbs for low and high beam. Xenon-equipped models use D1S 35W HID bulbs. Check the headlight lens for the xenon label, the under-hood sticker, or your owner manual to confirm before ordering.

profile.descriptionSuffix: C/K 1500 starter replacement for 5.7L V8 with solenoid testing and shim notes.

supportNote.title: 1996 Chevy 1500 starter jobs are about the shims and the heat shield
supportNote.intro: The GMT400 C/K 1500 with the 5.7L V8 uses a direct-drive starter mounted on the passenger side of the engine block. The factory heat shield protects the starter solenoid from exhaust manifold heat, but many replacement starters omit it. Without the heat shield, the solenoid contacts weld shut in hot weather.
bullets: Location: Passenger side of the engine block, directly below the exhaust manifold. Disconnect the battery negative terminal before working — the starter B+ terminal is always live.

Remove two 9/16-inch or 15 mm bolts holding the starter to the block. The top bolt is long and passes through the starter nose. Support the starter with one hand while removing the last bolt — it is heavy.

Check for shims between the starter nose and the block. These shims set the pinion-to-ring-gear clearance. If shims were present, reinstall them in the same positions. Missing shims cause grinding noises.

Test the old starter before condemning it. Many "bad" starters are actually caused by corroded battery terminals, bad ground cables, or a failing ignition switch. Verify 12V at the S terminal during crank before replacing.
Common part numbers: AC Delco 336-1925, Bosch SR8586X, Duralast 6494S. Always transfer the factory heat shield to the new starter if the replacement does not include one.

faq.question: Where is the starter on a 1996 Chevy 1500?
faq.answer: The starter is on the passenger side of the engine block, directly below the exhaust manifold. It is held by two bolts and has a heat shield to protect the solenoid from exhaust heat. Always disconnect the battery before working on the starter — the B+ terminal is live at all times.

profile.descriptionSuffix: 10th-gen Ford F-150 serpentine belt replacement for 4.6L and 5.4L Triton V8 engines.

supportNote.title: 1998 Ford F-150 belt routing is identical for the 4.6L and 5.4L Triton V8
supportNote.intro: The 10th-gen F-150 (1997-2003) uses a single serpentine belt with an automatic spring tensioner on both the 4.6L and 5.4L Triton V8 engines. The routing and belt length are the same, which simplifies parts ordering.
bullets: Use a 1/2-inch drive breaker bar inserted into the tensioner square drive. Rotate the tensioner CLOCKWISE to release belt tension. The tensioner is stiff — a short wrench will not provide enough leverage.
Routing: Crank → water pump → power steering → idler → alternator → A/C compressor → tensioner. The under-hood sticker shows the exact path.
Belt size: 6-rib, approximately 90.5 inches. Common part numbers: Motorcraft JK6-905A, Gates K060905, Dayco 5060905. Verify by matching the old belt length exactly.

Inspect the idler pulley and tensioner pulley while the belt is off. Spin them by hand. Any grinding, wobble, or roughness means replacement. The idler on Triton engines is a common 100K-mile failure.
Replace the tensioner if the arm chatters or the pulley bearing feels gritty. The factory tensioner uses a sealed bearing that cannot be serviced — replacement is the only fix.

faq.question: What size serpentine belt does a 1998 Ford F-150 5.4 take?
faq.answer: The 1998 Ford F-150 with the 5.4L Triton V8 uses a 6-rib serpentine belt approximately 90.5 inches long. Common part numbers include Motorcraft JK6-905A, Gates K060905, and Dayco 5060905. The 4.6L uses the same belt length and routing.

profile.titleSuffix: Cabin Air Filter Location & Replacement Guide
profile.descriptionSuffix: 11th-gen Ford F-150 cabin air filter replacement with location and part number guide.
extraKeywords: Ford F150 cabin air filter replacement

supportNote.title: 2007 Ford F-150 cabin filters hide behind the glove box and take 10 minutes to swap
supportNote.intro: The 11th-gen F-150 (2004-2008) places the cabin air filter behind the glove box on the passenger side. It is a straightforward replacement, but the glove box must be lowered to access the filter door. Many owners do not know the truck even has a cabin filter.
bullets: Open the glove box and remove the contents. Squeeze the sides of the glove box inward to release the stops, then lower it fully. The filter access door is directly behind it.

Remove the filter access door by pressing the two tabs and pulling it toward you. The old filter slides out horizontally. Note the airflow direction arrow on the old filter — the new one must face the same way.
Factory filter: Motorcraft FP-71 or FP-92 depending on HVAC configuration. Aftermarket: Fram CF10134, Bosch P3747, Wix 24003. Most retail chains stock at least one of these.
If the old filter is black with leaves and debris, check the cowl intake area under the hood for leaves. A clogged cowl grille forces the HVAC system to work harder and reduces airflow.

Replace the filter every 15,000-20,000 miles or annually. Trucks used on dirt roads or in pollen-heavy areas need more frequent changes. A clogged filter strains the blower motor and reduces defroster performance.

faq.question: Where is the cabin air filter on a 2007 Ford F-150?
faq.answer: The cabin air filter is behind the glove box on the passenger side. Lower the glove box by squeezing the sides inward, then remove the access door behind it. The filter slides out horizontally. Note the airflow direction arrow before removing the old filter.

profile.titleSuffix: Tail Light Bulb & Assembly Replacement Guide
profile.descriptionSuffix: 1st-gen Scion tC tail light bulb replacement with brake light, turn signal, and reverse light fitment.

supportNote.title: 2005 Scion tC tail light bulbs swap from inside the hatch — no tools needed
supportNote.intro: The first-gen Scion tC (2005-2010) is a liftback coupe with the tail light housings mounted on the hatch itself. All bulbs are accessible from inside the cargo area by removing small plastic access panels.
bullets: Open the hatch and locate the plastic access panels on the inner trim, directly behind each tail light. Pry them off with a flathead screwdriver — they pop out with light pressure.
Brake/tail light = 7443 dual-filament bulb. Turn signal = 7440 single-filament amber. Reverse light = 921. These are standard wedge-base bulbs available at any auto parts store.
Twist the bulb socket counter-clockwise to release it from the housing, then pull the old bulb straight out. Push the new bulb in firmly until it clicks. Do not touch the glass with bare fingers.
If the brake light works but the tail light does not (or vice versa), the 7443 bulb has two filaments. A single-filament 7440 will not work as a replacement — it only lights one circuit.
Check the hatch wiring harness where it passes through the rubber boot between the hatch and body. Constant flexing cracks wires and causes intermittent tail light failures on tCs.

faq.question: What tail light bulb does a 2005 Scion tC take?
faq.answer: The 2005 Scion tC uses a 7443 dual-filament bulb for the brake/tail light, a 7440 amber bulb for the turn signal, and a 921 bulb for the reverse light. All bulbs are accessible from inside the cargo area by removing the plastic access panels behind each tail light housing.

profile.descriptionSuffix: 8th-gen Chevrolet Malibu thermostat replacement for 2.5L Ecotec with torque specs and bleeding.

supportNote.title: 2014 Chevy Malibu thermostat jobs require a gentle touch — the housing is plastic
supportNote.intro: The 8th-gen Malibu (2013-2015) with the 2.5L Ecotec (LCV) has the thermostat integrated into a plastic housing on the driver side of the engine. The housing bolts to the cylinder head and includes the thermostat, seal, and temperature sensor in one assembly.
bullets: Location: Driver side of the engine, below the throttle body and behind the coolant surge tank. Remove the surge tank first for working room — two 10 mm bolts hold it to the strut tower.
The thermostat is sold as a complete housing assembly (AC Delco 15-81860 or Stant 14738). Do not try to separate the thermostat from the housing — it is permanently molded in.
Remove three 10 mm bolts holding the housing to the cylinder head. The lower bolt is the hardest to reach. Use a 1/4-inch drive ratchet with a short extension.
Torque the housing bolts to 89 lb-in (10 N·m) — not lb-ft. The plastic housing cracks if over-tightened. Use an inch-pound torque wrench for accuracy.

Refill with Dex-Cool or equivalent OAT coolant mixed 50/50. Bleed the system by opening the bleed screw on the coolant crossover pipe while filling. Air trapped in the heater core causes poor heat and overheating.

faq.question: Where is the thermostat on a 2014 Chevy Malibu?
faq.answer: The thermostat is inside a plastic housing on the driver side of the engine, below the throttle body. The housing bolts to the cylinder head with three 10 mm bolts. The thermostat is sold as a complete housing assembly — it cannot be separated from the housing.

profile.titleSuffix: Engine Air Filter Replacement & Fitment Guide
profile.descriptionSuffix: 7th-gen Nissan Sentra engine air filter location and replacement steps for 1.8L engine.

supportNote.title: 2014 Nissan Sentra air filters are a 3-minute swap with no tools
supportNote.intro: The B17 Sentra (2013-2019) with the 1.8L MRA8DE engine has a large rectangular air filter box on the driver side of the engine bay. The factory lid uses two spring clips — no screws or bolts. It is one of the easiest air filter jobs on any compact sedan.
bullets: Open the hood and locate the air filter box on the driver side, directly behind the headlight. The box lid has two metal spring clips on the front edge.
Release both clips and lift the lid straight up. The lid has alignment tabs on the rear edge — do not force it sideways or the tabs snap.
Remove the old filter and inspect the bottom of the box for debris. The Sentra intake sits low and sucks in leaves and dust from the road. Clean the box with a shop vacuum if needed.
Install the new filter with the pleats facing down and the rubber seal up. The filter should sit flush without gaps. Close the lid and snap both clips firmly.
Factory filter: Nissan 16546-3TA1B or equivalent. Aftermarket: Fram CA11114, Bosch S5537, K&N 33-2960 (washable). Change every 15,000-30,000 miles depending on driving conditions.

faq.question: Where is the engine air filter on a 2014 Nissan Sentra?
faq.answer: The engine air filter is in a rectangular box on the driver side of the engine bay, behind the headlight. Release two spring clips on the front of the lid, lift it off, and swap the filter. No tools are required.
